+msgid "# ACL Graph"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Overview"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Prerequisites and References"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Upstream vLLM design doc for generic graph concepts: [CUDA Graphs](https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/design/cuda_graphs/).\n"
+"- PyTorch graph documentation for generic capture and replay semantics: [Accelerating PyTorch with CUDA Graphs](https://pytorch.org/blog/accelerating-pytorch-with-cuda-graphs/).\n"
+"- Ascend user guide for operational enablement: [Graph Mode Guide](https://docs.vllm.ai/projects/ascend/en/latest/user_guide/feature_guide/graph_mode.html).\n"
+"- Existing repo design note: `docs/source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/ACL_Graph.md`."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## How ACL Graph Fits into vLLM"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- reading the runtime mode and `batch_descriptor` from the forward context,\n"
+"- deciding whether to run eagerly, capture a new ACL graph, or replay a cached ACL graph,\n"
+"- caching graph entries per batch descriptor,\n"
+"- preserving the graph pool and replay bookkeeping needed by the Ascend backend."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Capture Sizes and Bucketing"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `1`, `2`, `4`\n"
+"- multiples of `8` from `8` up to `255`\n"
+"- multiples of `16` from `256` up to `max_cudagraph_capture_size`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- sequence-parallel filtering may remove unsupported sizes,\n"
+"- runtime resource limits may still prevent some configured sizes from being captured,\n"
+"- some runtime modes may be normalized before capture begins."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Ascend-Specific Design Constraints"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Capture breadth is still constrained by runtime resources"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"### Platform mode normalization is stricter than generic upstream behavior"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Encoder-decoder models are forced to `PIECEWISE`.\n"
+"- `use_inductor` is disabled for ACL graph paths.\n"
+"- `ASCEND_LAUNCH_BLOCKING=1` is rejected when ACL graph is enabled.\n"
+"- Xlite graph mode can disable ACL graph full mode or fall back to `FULL_DECODE_ONLY`, depending on configuration."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Key Ascend-Specific Mechanisms"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Host-side attention parameter update for full graph replay"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. During capture, attention backends record per-graph task handles, events, workspaces, and weak references to the tensors or metadata that must be refreshed.\n"
+"2. Before replay, `update_full_graph_params()` calls the backend specific `update_graph_params()` implementation.\n"
+"3. That backend runs parameter refresh on an update stream with `torch.npu.graph_task_update_begin(...)` and `torch.npu.graph_task_update_end(...)` around the underlying attention operator launch.\n"
+"4. `torch.npu.ExternalEvent` objects are used to enforce ordering between the host-side update stream and the replay stream."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `vllm_ascend/attention/attention_v1.py`\n"
+"- `vllm_ascend/attention/mla_v1.py`\n"
+"- `vllm_ascend/attention/context_parallel/attention_cp.py`\n"
+"- `vllm_ascend/attention/context_parallel/mla_cp.py`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Replay ordering and synchronization"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Full vs Piecewise on Ascend"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Piecewise mode"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Full graph mode"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Diagnostics and Operational Notes"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- The simplest way to confirm that graph mode is active is to enable cudagraph metrics and keep log stats enabled. In CLI usage, use `--cudagraph-metrics` and do not pass `--disable-log-stats`. In Python usage, set `cudagraph_metrics=True` and `disable_log_stats=False`. Then inspect the emitted metrics and logs.\n"
+"- Profiling can also confirm whether replay is happening, and developers can add temporary prints before replay when debugging locally, but those are secondary methods and are not expanded here.\n"
+"- Capture-size selection primarily follows upstream configuration and dispatch behavior; only the confirmed stream-resource capture failure is rewritten with user-facing guidance at runtime.\n"
+"- In debug mode, `ACLGraphWrapper` asserts that replay uses the same tensor addresses recorded during capture.\n"
+"- `ASCEND_LAUNCH_BLOCKING=1` is incompatible with ACL graph enablement in the current implementation.\n"
+"- For debugging inside graph execution, the repo also provides graph-aware print helpers in `vllm_ascend.utils`, but those are developer diagnostics rather than part of the execution design."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Related Files"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `vllm_ascend/platform.py`, mode normalization, platform hooks, and static graph wrapper selection.\n"
+"- `vllm_ascend/compilation/acl_graph.py`, ACL graph wrapper, capture and replay cache, graph parameter containers, and full graph update dispatch.\n"
+"- `vllm_ascend/compilation/acl_graph.py`, runtime ACL graph capture, replay, and capture-failure guidance.\n"
+"- `vllm_ascend/attention/attention_v1.py`, full graph attention parameter capture and update logic.\n"
+"- `vllm_ascend/attention/mla_v1.py`, MLA (Multi-Head Latent Attention) specific full graph parameter capture and update logic.\n"
+"- `vllm_ascend/attention/context_parallel/attention_cp.py`, context parallel attention update path.\n"
+"- `vllm_ascend/attention/context_parallel/mla_cp.py`, context parallel MLA update path."
+msgstr ""
+

-#~ " degradation compared with eager mode."

+#~ "buckets in piecewise graph mode now, which will cause redundant computing "

+#~ "`torch.npu.graph_task_update_begin` 和 `torch.npu.graph_task_update_end` "
+#~ "来实现此功能，并使用 `torch.npu.ExternalEvent` 来确保参数更新与算子执行之间的顺序。"

 #~ msgid ""
-#~ "The current implementation uses a "

-#~ "waits for the update stream to "
-#~ "finish before replay begins. This is "
-#~ "simpler to reason about and sufficient"
-#~ " for current workloads. An asynchronous "
-#~ "pattern that overlaps update with replay"
-#~ " is possible in principle but not "
-#~ "yet implemented."
-#~ msgstr "当前实现使用同步更新模式：主机等待更新流完成后再开始重放。这样更容易推理，并且对于当前工作负载来说已经足够。异步模式在原则上可以重叠更新和重放，但尚未实现。"
+#~ "The current implementation uses a synchronous update pattern: the host waits"
+#~ " for the update stream to finish before replay begins. This is simpler to "
+#~ "reason about and sufficient for current workloads. An asynchronous pattern "
+#~ "that overlaps update with replay is possible in principle but not yet "
+#~ "implemented."
+#~ msgstr ""
+#~ "当前实现使用同步更新模式：主机等待更新流完成后再开始重放。这样更容易推理，并且对于当前工作负载来说已经足够。异步模式在原则上可以重叠更新和重放，但尚未实现。"

 #~ msgid ""
-#~ "Piecewise mode captures each model layer"
-#~ " as a separate ACL graph. This "
-#~ "reduces per-graph memory pressure and"
-#~ " makes it feasible to capture larger"
-#~ " batch sizes on Ascend. The trade-"
-#~ "off is that each layer incurs a"
-#~ " replay launch, so the host overhead"
-#~ " saved by graph mode is partially "
-#~ "offset by the number of segments."

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/KV_Cache_Pool_Guide.md:9
 msgid ""
 "Hence, KV Cache Pool is proposed to utilize various types of storage "
-"including on-chip memory, DRAM, and SSD, making a pool for KV Cache "
-"storage while making the prefix of requests visible across all nodes, "
-"increasing the cache hit rate for all requests."
+"including on-chip memory, DRAM, and SSD, making a pool for KV Cache storage "
+"while making the prefix of requests visible across all nodes, increasing the"
+" cache hit rate for all requests."

 "目前，我们仅对**预填充节点**执行 KV 池的 put 和 get 操作，而解码节点从 Mooncake P2P KV 连接器（即 "

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/KV_Cache_Pool_Guide.md:52
 msgid ""
-"The key benefit of doing this is that we can keep the gain in performance"
-" by computing less with Prefix Caching from on-chip memory and KV Pool "
-"for Prefill Nodes, while not sacrificing the data transfer efficiency "
-"between Prefill and Decode nodes with P2P KV Connector that transfers KV "
-"Caches between NPU devices directly."
+"The key benefit of doing this is that we can keep the gain in performance by"
+" computing less with Prefix Caching from on-chip memory and KV Pool for "
+"Prefill Nodes, while not sacrificing the data transfer efficiency between "
+"Prefill and Decode nodes with P2P KV Connector that transfers KV Caches "
+"between NPU devices directly."
 msgstr ""
-"这样做的主要好处是，通过片上内存和 KV "
-"池的前缀缓存减少预填充节点的计算量来保持性能提升，同时不会牺牲预填充节点和解码节点之间的数据传输效率——P2P KV 连接器直接在 NPU "
-"设备之间传输 KV 缓存。"
+"这样做的主要好处是，通过片上内存和 KV 池的前缀缓存减少预填充节点的计算量来保持性能提升，同时不会牺牲预填充节点和解码节点之间的数据传输效率——P2P"
+" KV 连接器直接在 NPU 设备之间传输 KV 缓存。"

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/KV_Cache_Pool_Guide.md:62
 msgid ""
-"MooncakeStoreConnectorV1 is also largely inspired by LMCacheConnectorV1 "
-"in terms of the `Lookup Engine`/`Lookup Client` design for looking up KV "
-"cache keys, and the `ChunkedTokenDatabase` class for processing tokens "
-"into prefix-aware hashes as well as other hashing related designs. On top"
-" of this, we have also added our own design including `KVTransferThread` "
-"that allows async `get` and `put` of KV caches with multi-threading, and "
-"NPU-related data transfer optimization such as removing the `LocalBuffer`"
-" in LMCache to remove redundant data transfer."
-msgstr ""
-"MooncakeStoreConnectorV1 在查找 KV 缓存键的 `Lookup Engine`/`Lookup Client` "
-"设计，以及将 token 处理为前缀感知哈希的 `ChunkedTokenDatabase` 类和其他哈希相关设计方面，也很大程度上受到了 "
+"MooncakeStoreConnectorV1 is also largely inspired by LMCacheConnectorV1 in "
+"terms of the `Lookup Engine`/`Lookup Client` design for looking up KV cache "
+"keys, and the `ChunkedTokenDatabase` class for processing tokens into "
+"prefix-aware hashes as well as other hashing related designs. On top of "
+"this, we have also added our own design including `KVTransferThread` that "
+"allows async `get` and `put` of KV caches with multi-threading, and NPU-"
+"related data transfer optimization such as removing the `LocalBuffer` in "
+"LMCache to remove redundant data transfer."
+msgstr ""
+"MooncakeStoreConnectorV1 在查找 KV 缓存键的 `Lookup Engine`/`Lookup Client` 设计，以及将 "
+"token 处理为前缀感知哈希的 `ChunkedTokenDatabase` 类和其他哈希相关设计方面，也很大程度上受到了 "
 "LMCacheConnectorV1 的启发。在此基础上，我们还添加了自己的设计，包括支持多线程异步 `get` 和 `put` KV 缓存的 "
 "`KVTransferThread`，以及与 NPU 相关的数据传输优化，例如移除 LMCache 中的 `LocalBuffer` "
 "以消除冗余数据传输。"

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/KV_Cache_Pool_Guide.md:91
 msgid ""
-"For now, if we successfully looked up a key and found it exists, but "
-"failed to get it when calling KV Pool's get function, we just output a "
-"log indicating the get operation failed and keep going; hence, the "
-"accuracy of that specific request may be affected. We will handle this "
-"situation by falling back the request and re-compute everything assuming "
-"there's no prefix cache hit (or even better, revert only one block and "
-"keep using the Prefix Caches before that)."
+"For now, if we successfully looked up a key and found it exists, but failed "
+"to get it when calling KV Pool's get function, we just output a log "
+"indicating the get operation failed and keep going; hence, the accuracy of "
+"that specific request may be affected. We will handle this situation by "
+"falling back the request and re-compute everything assuming there's no "
+"prefix cache hit (or even better, revert only one block and keep using the "
+"Prefix Caches before that)."
 msgstr ""
 "目前，如果我们成功查找到一个键并确认其存在，但在调用 KV 池的 get 函数时获取失败，我们仅输出一条日志表明 get "
 "操作失败并继续执行；因此，该特定请求的准确性可能会受到影响。我们将通过回退请求并假设没有前缀缓存命中来重新计算所有内容来处理这种情况（或者更优的方案是仅回退一个块，并继续使用该块之前的前缀缓存）。"
 
+msgid "# KV Cache Pool"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Why KV Cache Pool?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Usage"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## How it works?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 1. Combining KV Cache Pool with on-chip memory Prefix Caching"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Prefix Caching with on-chip memory is already supported by the vLLM V1 Engine.\n"
+"By introducing KV Connector V1, users can seamlessly combine on-chip memory-based Prefix Caching with Mooncake-backed KV Pool."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. The engine first checks for prefix hits in the on-chip memory cache."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"2. After getting the number of hit tokens on on-chip memory, it queries the "
+"KV Pool via the connector. If there are additional hits in the KV Pool, we "
+"get the **additional blocks only** from the KV Pool, and get the rest of the"
+" blocks directly from on-chip memory to minimize the data transfer latency."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"3. After the KV Caches in the KV Pool are loaded into on-chip memory, the "
+"remaining process is the same as Prefix Caching in on-chip memory."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 2. Combining KV Cache Pool with Mooncake PD Disaggregation"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## How is MooncakeStoreConnectorV1 Implemented?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### KV Connector Scheduler-Side Methods"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"`get_num_new_matched_tokens`: Get prefix cache hit in number of tokens through looking up into the KV pool.  \n"
+"`update_states_after_alloc`:  Update KVConnector state after temporary buffer alloc.  \n"
+"`build_connector_meta`: Attach the connector metadata to the request object.  \n"
+"`request_finished`: Once a request is finished, determine whether request blocks should be freed now or will be sent asynchronously and freed later."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Connector Worker-Side Methods"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"`register_kv_caches`: Register KV cache buffers needed for KV cache transfer.\n"
+"`start_load_kv`: Perform KV cache load operation that transfers KV cache from storage to device.\n"
+"`wait_for_layer_load`: Optional; Wait for layer load in layerwise + async KV load scenario.\n"
+"`save_kv_layer`: Optional; Do layerwise KV cache put into KV Pool.\n"
+"`wait_for_save`: Wait for KV Save to finish if async KV cache save/put.\n"
+"`get_finished`: Get request that finished KV transfer, `done_sending` if `put` finished, `done_receiving` if `get` finished."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## DFX"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. When looking up a key in KV Pool, if we cannot find the key, there is no Cache Hit for this specific block; we return no hit for this block and do not look up further blocks for the current request.\n"
+"2. Similarly, when we are trying to put a block into KV Pool and it fails, we do not put further blocks (subject to change)."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Limitations"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. Currently, MooncakeStore for vLLM-Ascend only supports DRAM as the "
+"storage for KV Cache pool."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"2. For now, if we successfully looked up a key and found it exists, but "
+"failed to get it when calling KV Pool's get function, we just output a log "
+"indicating the get operation failed and keep going; hence, the accuracy of "
+"that specific request may be affected. We will handle this situation by "
+"falling back the request and re-compute everything assuming there's no "
+"prefix cache hit (or even better, revert only one block and keep using the "
+"Prefix Caches before that)."
+msgstr ""
+
 #~ msgid ""
-#~ "For details, please also refer to "
-#~ "the Mooncake Connector Store Deployment "
+#~ "For details, please also refer to the Mooncake Connector Store Deployment "
 #~ "Guide."
 #~ msgstr "有关详细信息，请参阅 Mooncake Connector Store 部署指南。"

-"`[0 * 6 + 0 / 2, 0 * 6 + 1 / 2, 0 * 6 + 2 / 2, 1 * 6 + 0 / 2, 1 * 6 + 1 /"

-"14]`。这可用于从 `block table` 中选择 `device block number`。"

+"1 * 6 + 0 / 2, 1 * 6 + 1 / 2, 2 * 6 + 0 / 2, 2 * 6 + 1 / 2, 2 * 6 + 2 / 2, 2"

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/ModelRunner_prepare_inputs.md:286
 msgid ""
-"Hope this document helps you better understand how vLLM prepares inputs "
-"for model forwarding. If you have any good ideas, you are welcome to "
-"contribute to us."
+"Hope this document helps you better understand how vLLM prepares inputs for "
+"model forwarding. If you have any good ideas, you are welcome to contribute "
+"to us."
 msgstr "希望本文档能帮助你更好地理解 vLLM 如何为模型前向传播准备输入。如果你有任何好的想法，欢迎向我们贡献。"
+
+msgid "# Prepare inputs for model forwarding"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Purpose"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- the inputs\n"
+"- the corresponding attention metadata of the inputs"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Overview"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 1. Obtain inputs"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. Get `token positions`: relative position of each token within its request"
+" sequence."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"2. Get `token indices`: index of each scheduled token in the token table."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"3. Get `Token IDs`: using token indices to retrieve the Token IDs from "
+"**token id table**."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 2. Build inputs attention metadata"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `query start location`: start and end location of each request corresponding to the scheduled tokens.\n"
+"- `sequence length`: length of each request including both computed tokens and newly scheduled tokens.\n"
+"- `number of computed tokens`: number of computed tokens for each request.\n"
+"- `number of requests`: number of requests in this batch.\n"
+"- `number of tokens`: total number of scheduled tokens in this batch.\n"
+"- **`block table`**: translates the logical address (within its sequence) of each block to its global physical address in the device's memory.\n"
+"- `max query len`: the longest scheduled tokens length in this request batch.\n"
+"- `slot mapping`: indices of each token that input token will be stored into.\n"
+"- `attention mask`: mask matrix applied to attention scores before softmax to control which tokens can attend to each other (usually a causal attention)."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Before start"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- token level: represents one attribute corresponding to each scheduled token, so the length of this variable is the number of scheduled tokens.\n"
+"- request level: represents one attribute of each scheduled request, whose length usually is the number of scheduled requests. (`query start location` is a special case, which has one more element.)\n"
+"- system level:\n"
+"  1. **Token IDs table**: stores the token IDs (i.e. the inputs of a model) of each request. The shape of this table is `(max num request, max model len)`. Here, `max num request` is the maximum count of concurrent requests allowed in a forward batch and `max model len` is the maximum token count that can be handled at one request sequence in this model.\n"
+"  2. **Block table**: translates the logical address (within its sequence) of each block to its global physical address in the device's memory. The shape of this table is `(max num request, max model len / block size)`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Tips"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Simply put, a `token ID` is an **integer** (usually `int32`), which represents a token.\n"
+"Example of `Token ID`:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Go through details"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- maximum number of tokens that can be scheduled at once: 10\n"
+"- `block size`: 2\n"
+"- Totally schedule 3 requests. Their prompt lengths are 3, 2, and 8 respectively.\n"
+"- `max model length`: 12 (the maximum token count that can be handled at one request sequence in a model)."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Step 1: All requests in the prefill phase"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### Obtain inputs"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "##### 1. Get token positions"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "##### 2. Get token indices"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- We fill all Token IDs in one request sequence to this table at once, but "
+"we only retrieve the tokens we scheduled this time. Then we retrieve the "
+"remaining Token IDs next time."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "##### 3. Retrieve the Token IDs"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `Input IDs` = `[T_0_0, T_0_1, T_0_2, T_1_0, T_1_1, T_2_0, T_2_1, T_3_2, "
+"T_3_3, T_3_4]`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### Build inputs attention metadata"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. Get `block table indices` using `K`, `positions` and `request indices`."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "2. Get `device block number` using `block table indices`."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "3. Get `block offsets` using `positions` and `block size`."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"4. construct `slot mapping` using `device block number` and `block offsets`."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. (**Token level**) Use a simple formula to calculate `block table indices`: `request indices * K + positions / block size`. So it equals `[0 * 6 + 0 / 2, 0 * 6 + 1 / 2, 0 * 6 + 2 / 2, 1 * 6 + 0 / 2, 1 * 6 + 1 / 2, 2 * 6 + 0 / 2, 2 * 6 + 1 / 2, 2 * 6 + 2 / 2, 2 * 6 + 3 / 2, 2 * 6 + 4 / 2] = [0, 0, 1, 6, 6, 12, 12, 13, 13, 14]`. This could be used to select `device block number` from `block table`.\n"
+"2. (**Token level**) Use `block table indices` to select out `device block number` for each scheduled token. The pseudocode is `block_numbers = block_table[block_table_indices]`. So `device block number=[1, 1, 2, 3, 3, 4, 4, 5, 5, 6]`\n"
+"3. (**Token level**) `block offsets` could be computed by `block offsets = positions % block size = [0, 1, 0, 0, 1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 0]`.\n"
+"4. Finally, use `block offsets` and `device block number` to create `slot mapping`: `device block number * block size + block_offsets = [2, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12]`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- (**Request level**) Use prefix sum to calculate `query start location`: `[0, 3, 5, 10]`.\n"
+"- (**Request level**) All tokens in step 1 are in the prefill stage, and the computed tokens count is 0; then `sequence length` = `[3, 2, 5]`.\n"
+"- (**Request level**) As mentioned above, `number of computed tokens` are all 0s: `[0, 0, 0]`.\n"
+"- `number of requests`: `3`\n"
+"- (**Request level**) `number of tokens`: `[3, 2, 5]`\n"
+"- `max query len`: `5`\n"
+"- (**Token level**) `slot mapping`: `[2, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12]`\n"
+"- `attention mask`: For all requests that initiate a prefill process, we simply create only one mask matrix for reuse across different requests. The shape of this mask matrix is `5 * 5`:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Step 2: Chunked prefill"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. `request indices`: `[0, 1, 2, 2, 2]`\n"
+"2. `token positions`: `[3, 2, 5, 6, 7]`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"3. `token indices`: `[3, 14, 29, 30, 31]`\n"
+"4. `Input IDs`: `[T_0_3, T_1_2, T_3_5, T_3_6, T_3_7]`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. (**Token level**) `block table indices`: `[1, 7, 14, 15, 15]`\n"
+"2. (**Token level**) `device block number`: `[2, 7, 6, 8, 8]`\n"
+"3. (**Token level**) `block offsets`: `[1, 0, 1, 0, 1]`\n"
+"4. (**Token level**) `slot mapping`: `[5, 14, 13, 16, 17]`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "- `query start location`: `[0, 1, 2, 5]`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "- `sequence length`: `[4, 3, 8]`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "- `number of computed tokens`: `[3, 2, 5]`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "- `number of requests`: `3`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "- `max query len`: `3`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "- `slot mapping`: `[5, 14, 13, 16, 17]`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "- `attention mask`: `5 * 8`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## At last"
+msgstr ""

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/context_parallel.md:9
 msgid "What is CP?"
 msgstr "什么是 CP？"
 
 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/context_parallel.md:11
 msgid ""
-"**Context Parallel (CP)** is a strategy for parallelizing computation "
-"along the sequence dimension across multiple devices."
-msgstr ""
-"**上下文并行（Context Parallel，CP）** 是一种在多个设备上沿序列维度进行并行计算的策略。"
+"**Context Parallel (CP)** is a strategy for parallelizing computation along "
+"the sequence dimension across multiple devices."
+msgstr "**上下文并行（Context Parallel，CP）** 是一种在多个设备上沿序列维度进行并行计算的策略。"
 
 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/context_parallel.md:13
 msgid ""
 "**Prefill Context Parallel (PCP)** expands the world size of devices and "
-"uses dedicated communication domains. Its primary goal is to partition "
-"the sequence dimension during the prefill phase, enabling different "
-"devices to compute distinct chunks of the sequence simultaneously. The KV"
-" cache is sharded along the sequence dimension across devices. This "
-"approach impacts the computational logic of both the Prefill and Decode "
-"stages to varying degrees."
+"uses dedicated communication domains. Its primary goal is to partition the "
+"sequence dimension during the prefill phase, enabling different devices to "
+"compute distinct chunks of the sequence simultaneously. The KV cache is "
+"sharded along the sequence dimension across devices. This approach impacts "
+"the computational logic of both the Prefill and Decode stages to varying "
+"degrees."
 msgstr ""
 "**预填充上下文并行（Prefill Context Parallel，PCP）** "
 "扩展了设备的世界规模，并使用专用的通信域。其主要目标是在预填充阶段对序列维度进行切分，使不同设备能够同时计算序列的不同块。KV "
 "缓存沿序列维度分片存储到不同设备上。这种方法在不同程度上影响了预填充和解码阶段的计算逻辑。"
 
 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/context_parallel.md:18
 msgid ""
-"**Decode Context Parallel (DCP)** reuses the communication domain of "
-"Tensor Parallelism (TP) and does not require additional devices. Its main"
-" objective is to eliminate duplicated storage of the KV cache by sharding"
-" it along the sequence dimension across devices within the TP domain that"
-" would otherwise hold redundant copies. DCP primarily influences the "
-"Decode logic, as well as the logic for chunked prefill and cached "
-"prefill."
+"**Decode Context Parallel (DCP)** reuses the communication domain of Tensor "
+"Parallelism (TP) and does not require additional devices. Its main objective"
+" is to eliminate duplicated storage of the KV cache by sharding it along the"
+" sequence dimension across devices within the TP domain that would otherwise"
+" hold redundant copies. DCP primarily influences the Decode logic, as well "
+"as the logic for chunked prefill and cached prefill."
 msgstr ""
-"**解码上下文并行（Decode Context Parallel，DCP）** "
-"复用张量并行（TP）的通信域，不需要额外的设备。其主要目标是通过将 KV 缓存沿序列维度分片到 TP 域内的设备上（原本会存储冗余副本），消除 "
-"KV 缓存的重复存储。DCP 主要影响解码逻辑，以及分块预填充和缓存预填充的逻辑。"
+"**解码上下文并行（Decode Context Parallel，DCP）** 复用张量并行（TP）的通信域，不需要额外的设备。其主要目标是通过将 "
+"KV 缓存沿序列维度分片到 TP 域内的设备上（原本会存储冗余副本），消除 KV 缓存的重复存储。DCP "
+"主要影响解码逻辑，以及分块预填充和缓存预填充的逻辑。"

 msgstr ""
 "**解码阶段：** 解码阶段的逻辑与 GQA 的分块预填充一致：首先沿 Q 头维度执行 all-gather 操作，以确保 DCP "

@@ -288,19 +285,18 @@ msgstr "**首尾风格的 Token 切分**"
 msgid ""
 "PCP requires splitting the input sequence and ensuring balanced "
 "computational load across devices during the prefill phase. We employ a "
-"head-tail style for splitting and concatenation: specifically, the "
-"sequence is first padded to a length of `2*pcp_size`, then divided into "
-"`2*pcp_size` equal parts. The first part is merged with the last part, "
-"the second part with the second last part, and so on, thereby assigning "
-"computationally balanced chunks to each device. Additionally, since "
-"allgather aggregation of KV or Q results in interleaved chunks from "
-"different requests, we compute `pcp_allgather_restore_idx` to quickly "
-"restore the original order."
+"head-tail style for splitting and concatenation: specifically, the sequence "
+"is first padded to a length of `2*pcp_size`, then divided into `2*pcp_size` "
+"equal parts. The first part is merged with the last part, the second part "
+"with the second last part, and so on, thereby assigning computationally "
+"balanced chunks to each device. Additionally, since allgather aggregation of"
+" KV or Q results in interleaved chunks from different requests, we compute "
+"`pcp_allgather_restore_idx` to quickly restore the original order."
 msgstr ""
 "PCP 需要在预填充阶段切分输入序列，并确保各设备间的计算负载均衡。我们采用首尾风格进行切分与拼接：具体地，首先将序列填充到长度为 "
 "`2*pcp_size`，然后等分为 `2*pcp_size` "
-"份。第一部分与最后一部分合并，第二部分与倒数第二部分合并，依此类推，从而为每个设备分配计算负载均衡的块。此外，由于 KV 或 Q 的 "
-"allgather 聚合会导致来自不同请求的块交错，我们计算 `pcp_allgather_restore_idx` 以快速恢复原始顺序。"
+"份。第一部分与最后一部分合并，第二部分与倒数第二部分合并，依此类推，从而为每个设备分配计算负载均衡的块。此外，由于 KV 或 Q 的 allgather"
+" 聚合会导致来自不同请求的块交错，我们计算 `pcp_allgather_restore_idx` 以快速恢复原始顺序。"

 "During the Prefill phase (excluding chunked prefill), we employ an all-"
 "gather KV approach to address the issue of incomplete sequences on "
 "individual GPUs. It is important to note that we only aggregate the KV "
-"values for the current layer at a time, and these are discarded "
-"immediately after use, avoiding excessive peak memory usage. This method "
-"can also be directly applied to KV cache storage (since the KV cache "
-"partitioning method differs from PCP sequence partitioning, it is "
-"inevitable that each GPU requires a complete copy of the KV values). All "
-"attention backends maintain consistency in this logic."
+"values for the current layer at a time, and these are discarded immediately "
+"after use, avoiding excessive peak memory usage. This method can also be "
+"directly applied to KV cache storage (since the KV cache partitioning method"
+" differs from PCP sequence partitioning, it is inevitable that each GPU "
+"requires a complete copy of the KV values). All attention backends maintain "
+"consistency in this logic."
 msgstr ""
-"在预填充阶段（不包括分块预填充），我们采用 all-gather KV 方法来解决单个 GPU "
-"上序列不完整的问题。需要注意的是，我们每次只聚合当前层的 KV 值，使用后立即丢弃，避免了过高的峰值内存使用。该方法也可以直接应用于 KV "
-"缓存存储（由于 KV 缓存切分方式与 PCP 序列切分不同，每个 GPU 不可避免地需要完整的 KV 值副本）。所有注意力后端在此逻辑上保持一致。"
+"在预填充阶段（不包括分块预填充），我们采用 all-gather KV 方法来解决单个 GPU 上序列不完整的问题。需要注意的是，我们每次只聚合当前层的"
+" KV 值，使用后立即丢弃，避免了过高的峰值内存使用。该方法也可以直接应用于 KV 缓存存储（由于 KV 缓存切分方式与 PCP 序列切分不同，每个 "
+"GPU 不可避免地需要完整的 KV 值副本）。所有注意力后端在此逻辑上保持一致。"

-"were limited."

-"gather KV 实现。"
+"注意：虽然 Ring Attention 方法也可以以更低的峰值内存实现信息交换并支持计算-"

+"compatibility: **AllGatherQ**, **AllGatherKV**, and **Ring-Attn**. Since PCP"
+" performs sequence sharding on both the query sequence and the KV cache, we "

+"like Ring-Attn to perform computations sequentially. The advantages and "
+"disadvantages of Ring-Attn will not be elaborated here."

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/context_parallel.md:132

-"forward, each rank all-gathers the TP-sharded `o_proj` weight and all "
-"input-sharded quantization parameters into temporary full-weight buffers."
-" The full-weight `o_proj` forward runs once for that batch, and the "
-"module is then restored to the TP parameter aliases."

+"sharded quantization parameters into temporary full-weight buffers. The "
+"full-weight `o_proj` forward runs once for that batch, and the module is "
+"then restored to the TP parameter aliases."
 msgstr ""
 "**预填充或混合批次**产生的 SFA 输出与 TP 分片 `o_proj` 的输入布局不直接兼容。在 `o_proj` 前向传播之前，每个 rank "
-"将 TP 分片的 `o_proj` 权重和所有输入分片的量化参数 all-gather 到临时的全权重缓冲区中。全权重 `o_proj` 前向传播对该批次运行一次，然后模块恢复到"
-" TP 参数别名。"
+"将 TP 分片的 `o_proj` 权重和所有输入分片的量化参数 all-gather 到临时的全权重缓冲区中。全权重 `o_proj` "

-"reusable communication buffers for prefill/mixed full-gather execution "

-"张量是仅用于预填充/混合全收集执行的可重用通信缓冲区。它们不得成为 TP 权重的第二个持久化副本。"
+"存储不变性在于原始的 TP 分片 `o_proj` 参数仍然是唯一的持久化真实来源。`o_proj_tp_*` "

+"权重的第二个持久化副本。"

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/context_parallel.md:151

 msgstr "SFA 后端：`vllm_ascend/attention/context_parallel/sfa_cp.py`"
+
+msgid "# Context Parallel (CP)"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## What is CP?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"**Prefill Context Parallel (PCP)** expands the world size of devices and uses dedicated communication domains.\n"
+"Its primary goal is to partition the sequence dimension during the prefill phase, enabling different devices to compute distinct chunks of the sequence simultaneously.\n"
+"The KV cache is sharded along the sequence dimension across devices.\n"
+"This approach impacts the computational logic of both the Prefill and Decode stages to varying degrees."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"**Decode Context Parallel (DCP)** reuses the communication domain of Tensor Parallelism (TP) and does not require additional devices.\n"
+"Its main objective is to eliminate duplicated storage of the KV cache by sharding it along the sequence dimension across devices within the TP domain that would otherwise hold redundant copies.\n"
+"DCP primarily influences the Decode logic, as well as the logic for chunked prefill and cached prefill."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## How to Use CP?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## How It Works?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Device Distribution"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"We introduce new communication domains for PCP and reuse TP for DCP, and this is the new layout of devices for PCP2, DCP2, and TP4.\n"
+"![device_world](../../assets/cp/device_world.png)"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Block Table"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"For any token `x`, referencing the following figure, its (virtual) block index is `x // virtual_block_size`, and the offset within the virtual block is `offset_within_virtual_block = x % virtual_block_size`.\n"
+"The local block index is `local_block_index = offset_within_virtual_block // cp_kv_cache_interleave_size`, and the device number is `target_rank = local_block_index % cp_size`.\n"
+"The offset within the local block is `(local_block_index // cp_size) * cp_kv_cache_interleave_size + offset_within_virtual_block % cp_kv_cache_interleave_size`."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Decode Context Parallel (DCP)"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"**Prefill Phase:**  \n"
+"As illustrated, during the Chunked Prefill computation, two distinct logic implementations are employed for MLA and GQA backends."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- In the **MLA backend**, a Context KV Cache `all_gather` operation is performed to aggregate the full KV values.\n"
+"These are then used for attention computation with the Q values of the current chunk.\n"
+"Note that in multi-request scenarios, the directly gathered KV results are interleaved across requests.\n"
+"The `reorg_kvcache` function is used to reorganize the KV cache, ensuring that the KV cache of the same request is stored contiguously."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- In the **GQA backend**, an `all_gather` is performed along the head dimension for Q.\n"
+"This is because DCP overlaps with the TP communication domain, and the Q heads within a DCP group differ.\n"
+"However, they need to exchange results with the locally computed KV cache for online Softmax updates.\n"
+"To ensure correctness during result updates, the Q values are synchronized across the DCP group via head-dimension `all_gather`.\n"
+"During the result update process, `cp_lse_ag_out_rs` is invoked to aggregate `attn_output` and `attn_lse`, update the results, and perform a reduce-scatter operation on the outputs.\n"
+"Alternatively, we can use an all-to-all communication to exchange the output and LSE results, followed by direct local updates. This approach aligns with the logic adapted for PCP compatibility."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"**Decode Phase:**\n"
+"The logic during the decode phase is consistent with that of GQA's chunked prefill: an all-gather operation is first performed along the Q head dimension to ensure consistency within the DCP group.\n"
+"After computing the results with the local KV cache, the results are updated via the `cp_lse_ag_out_rs` function."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Prefill Context Parallel (PCP)"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"PCP requires splitting the input sequence and ensuring balanced computational load across devices during the prefill phase.\n"
+"We employ a head-tail style for splitting and concatenation: specifically, the sequence is first padded to a length of `2*pcp_size`, then divided into `2*pcp_size` equal parts.\n"
+"The first part is merged with the last part, the second part with the second last part, and so on, thereby assigning computationally balanced chunks to each device.\n"
+"Additionally, since allgather aggregation of KV or Q results in interleaved chunks from different requests, we compute `pcp_allgather_restore_idx` to quickly restore the original order."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"During the Prefill phase (excluding chunked prefill), we employ an all-gather KV approach to address the issue of incomplete sequences on individual GPUs.\n"
+"It is important to note that we only aggregate the KV values for the current layer at a time, and these are discarded immediately after use, avoiding excessive peak memory usage.\n"
+"This method can also be directly applied to KV cache storage (since the KV cache partitioning method differs from PCP sequence partitioning, it is inevitable that each GPU requires a complete copy of the KV values).\n"
+"All attention backends maintain consistency in this logic."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Currently, there are three viable approaches for Chunked Prefill compatibility: **AllGatherQ**, **AllGatherKV**, and **Ring-Attn**.\n"
+"Since PCP performs sequence sharding on both the query sequence and the KV cache, we need to ensure that one side has complete information or employ a method like Ring-Attn to perform computations sequentially.\n"
+"The advantages and disadvantages of Ring-Attn will not be elaborated here."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"We have implemented the **AllGatherQ** approach in the GQA attention backend and the **AllGatherKV** approach in the MLA attention backend.\n"
+"The workflow after **AllGatherQ** is identical to the decode phase, while the workflow after **AllGatherKV** is the same as the standard prefill phase.\n"
+"For details, please refer to the diagram below; specific steps will not be repeated."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"One important note: **AllGatherKV** may lead to significant peak memory usage when the context length becomes excessively long.\n"
+"To mitigate this, we adopt a segmented processing strategy.\n"
+"By predefining the maximum amount of KV cache processed per round, we sequentially complete the attention computation and online softmax updates for each segment."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### SFA DSA-CP Mixed `o_proj` Path"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"SFA DSA-CP mixed execution intentionally reuses the normal TP-sharded `o_proj`.\n"
+"This is part of the DSA-CP mixed data path, not a standalone user-facing `o_proj` TP switch.\n"
+"The mixed path is used when one instance may handle both decode-only and prefill/mixed batches, so `o_proj` must support two layouts at runtime:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- **Decode-only batches** keep the decode TP path.\n"
+"  SFA outputs are exchanged with an all-to-all in the TP group, then the original TP-sharded `o_proj` runs normally.\n"
+"- **Prefill or mixed batches** produce SFA outputs that are not directly compatible with the TP-sharded `o_proj` input layout.\n"
+"  Before `o_proj` forward, each rank all-gathers the TP-sharded `o_proj` weight and all input-sharded quantization parameters into temporary full-weight buffers.\n"
+"  The full-weight `o_proj` forward runs once for that batch, and the module is then restored to the TP parameter aliases."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"The storage invariant is that the original TP-sharded `o_proj` parameter remains the only persistent source of truth.\n"
+"`o_proj_tp_*` tensors are aliases of the original parameter storage.\n"
+"`o_proj_full_*` tensors are reusable communication buffers for prefill/mixed full-gather execution only.\n"
+"They must not become a second persistent copy of the TP weight."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Related Files"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- slot_mapping computation: `vllm_ascend/worker/block_table.py`\n"
+"- sequences splitting and metadata prepare: `vllm_ascend/worker/model_runner_v1.py`\n"
+"- PCP token splitting and metadata generation: `vllm_ascend/worker/pcp_utils.py`\n"
+"- GQA backend: `vllm_ascend/attention/context_parallel/attention_cp.py`\n"
+"- MLA backend: `vllm_ascend/attention/context_parallel/mla_cp.py`\n"
+"- DSA backend: `vllm_ascend/attention/context_parallel/dsa_cp.py`\n"
+"- SFA backend: `vllm_ascend/attention/context_parallel/sfa_cp.py`"
+msgstr ""

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/cpu_binding.md:9
 msgid ""
-"The feature does not change model execution logic or numerical results. "
-"It only controls CPU placement for the worker process, key runtime "
-"threads, memory pages, and NPU IRQs when the host environment allows it. "
-"By keeping the main worker, ACL, and release threads on dedicated CPU "
-"ranges, it **helps reduce context-switch overhead from scheduler "
-"preemption on busy hosts.**"
+"The feature does not change model execution logic or numerical results. It "
+"only controls CPU placement for the worker process, key runtime threads, "
+"memory pages, and NPU IRQs when the host environment allows it. By keeping "
+"the main worker, ACL, and release threads on dedicated CPU ranges, it "
+"**helps reduce context-switch overhead from scheduler preemption on busy "
+"hosts.**"
 msgstr ""

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/cpu_binding.md:17
 msgid ""
-"On multi-socket ARM systems, the Linux scheduler may place worker threads"
-" on CPUs far from the NPU that the worker drives. This can increase "
-"cross-NUMA traffic, increase thread preemption, and introduce latency "
-"jitter. The Ascend backend therefore owns a CPU allocation policy to "
-"**reduce cross-NUMA traffic, reduce thread preemption, and improve "
-"latency stability** instead of relying on upstream GPU NUMA binding "
-"flags."

+" CPUs far from the NPU that the worker drives. This can increase cross-NUMA "
+"traffic, increase thread preemption, and introduce latency jitter. The "
+"Ascend backend therefore owns a CPU allocation policy to **reduce cross-NUMA"
+" traffic, reduce thread preemption, and improve latency stability** instead "
+"of relying on upstream GPU NUMA binding flags."

 "流量、增加线程抢占并引入延迟抖动。因此，Ascend 后端拥有自己的 CPU 分配策略，旨在**减少跨 NUMA "
@@ -73,17 +72,17 @@ msgstr "这也是上游 NUMA 标志在 Ascend 上被适配的原因："

 "这是关键特性：两个独立的工作进程，即使具有相同的 cpuset 但不同的可见 NPU ID，仍然获得**不重叠的 CPU "

+"池**，因为两个进程都针对相同的全局 NPU ID 空间进行分片。使用 NUMA 对齐的 cpuset，这也提供了**工作进程间的 CPU/NUMA "

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/cpu_binding.md:93
 msgid ""
-"`topo_affinity` is designed for A2, Atlas 300 inference products, and "
-"other non-A3 device types. A2 and Atlas 300 inference products expose "
-"**meaningful NPU-to-CPU affinity information**, so the allocator starts "

+"non-A3 device types. A2 and Atlas 300 inference products expose **meaningful"
+" NPU-to-CPU affinity information**, so the allocator starts from NPU "
+"topology affinity when it is available and then avoids overlap for shared "
+"affinity groups."

@@ -840,3 +840,280 @@ msgstr ""
 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/cpu_binding.md:256
 msgid "Tests: `tests/ut/device_allocator/test_cpu_binding.py`"
 msgstr "测试：`tests/ut/device_allocator/test_cpu_binding.py`"
+
+msgid "# CPU Binding"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Overview"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"CPU binding is an **Ascend-native host-side optimization** for vLLM workers on\n"
+"ARM servers. **Starting from vllm-ascend v0.18.0rc1, it is enabled by default\n"
+"through `enable_cpu_binding=True`.**"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"The feature does not change model execution logic or numerical results. It only\n"
+"controls CPU placement for the worker process, key runtime threads, memory\n"
+"pages, and NPU IRQs when the host environment allows it. By keeping the main\n"
+"worker, ACL, and release threads on dedicated CPU ranges, it **helps reduce\n"
+"context-switch overhead from scheduler preemption on busy hosts.**"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Why CPU Binding?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"On multi-socket ARM systems, the Linux scheduler may place worker threads on\n"
+"CPUs far from the NPU that the worker drives. This can increase cross-NUMA\n"
+"traffic, increase thread preemption, and introduce latency jitter. The Ascend\n"
+"backend therefore owns a CPU allocation policy to **reduce cross-NUMA traffic,\n"
+"reduce thread preemption, and improve latency stability** instead of relying on\n"
+"upstream GPU NUMA binding flags."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `--numa-bind` is converted to `additional_config={\"enable_cpu_binding\": true}`.\n"
+"- `--numa-bind-nodes` and `--numa-bind-cpus` are ignored because Ascend computes CPU pools from NPU topology or global logical NPU IDs."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## How It Works?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Input | Source | Purpose |\n"
+"| --- | --- | --- |\n"
+"| Allowed CPUs | `/proc/self/status` `Cpus_allowed_list` | The only CPUs eligible for binding. Container cpusets are respected. |\n"
+"| Logical NPU map | `npu-smi info -m` | Maps card/chip IDs to global logical NPU IDs and gives `total_logic_npus`. On Ascend 950, `Chip Logic ID` is not reported, so `NPU ID` is used as the logical ID. |\n"
+"| Running NPUs | `npu-smi info` process table, filtered by `ASCEND_RT_VISIBLE_DEVICES` | Identifies the logical NPUs used by this worker process. A2/A3 process rows use `NPU Chip`; Ascend 950 process rows use `NPU ID`. |\n"
+"| Topology affinity | `npu-smi info -t topo` | Provides NPU-to-CPU affinity for `topo_affinity` mode. |\n"
+"| CPU NUMA map | `lscpu -e=CPU,NODE` | Used to extend single-NUMA affinity pools to the next NUMA node. |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Strategy Selection"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Device type | Strategy | Reason |\n"
+"| --- | --- | --- |\n"
+"| A3 | `global_slice` | A3 uses HCCS card-to-card interconnect. Each NPU is nearly equidistant from all NUMA nodes, so there is no strong NPU-to-NUMA affinity signal. Global logical NPU ID based slicing gives deterministic, non-overlapping CPU pools and CPU/NUMA isolation between workers. |\n"
+"| Ascend 950 | `global_slice` | Ascend 950 reports NPU-to-NPU/NIC topology but does not report NPU-to-CPU affinity in `npu-smi info -t topo`. It also reports process rows by `NPU ID` instead of `NPU Chip`. Global logical NPU ID based slicing keeps CPU pools deterministic without relying on missing affinity data. |\n"
+"| A2 and Atlas 300 inference products | `topo_affinity` | A2 and Atlas 300 inference products provide NPU-to-CPU affinity information through `npu-smi info -t topo`, so they use this topology signal when available. |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### CPU Pool Construction"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### global_slice"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"`global_slice` is designed for devices without a useful NPU-to-CPU affinity\n"
+"signal, including A3 and Ascend 950. Because A3's **HCCS interconnect makes the distance\n"
+"from each NPU to each NUMA node nearly the same**, topology affinity is not a\n"
+"useful placement signal. Ascend 950 similarly exposes UB/NIC topology but not CPU\n"
+"affinity. The allocator therefore partitions the sorted `allowed_cpus` list by\n"
+"global logical NPU ID."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. Determine `total_npus` in this order:\n"
+"   - `total_logic_npus` from `npu-smi info -m`\n"
+"   - number of topo affinity entries\n"
+"   - number of running NPUs\n"
+"2. Compute:\n"
+"   - `base = len(allowed_cpus) // total_npus`\n"
+"   - `extra = len(allowed_cpus) % total_npus`\n"
+"3. Each logical NPU gets a deterministic slice:\n"
+"   - NPU IDs `< extra` receive `base + 1` CPUs.\n"
+"   - Remaining NPU IDs receive `base` CPUs.\n"
+"4. Only running NPUs are materialized into `npu_cpu_pool`."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"This is the key property: two independent worker processes with the same cpuset\n"
+"but different visible NPU IDs still get **non-overlapping CPU pools** because\n"
+"both processes slice against the same global NPU ID space. With a NUMA-aligned\n"
+"cpuset, this also provides **CPU/NUMA isolation between workers**, so one worker\n"
+"does not share the same CPU or NUMA slice with another worker."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Devices with IRQ binding require `base >= 5`:\n"
+"  2 CPUs for SQ/CQ IRQ binding, at least 1 CPU for the main worker, 1 CPU for\n"
+"  ACL thread, and 1 CPU for release thread.\n"
+"- Ascend 950 skips IRQ binding and does not reserve SQ/CQ IRQ CPUs, so it requires\n"
+"  `base >= 3`: at least 1 CPU for the main worker, 1 CPU for ACL thread, and\n"
+"  1 CPU for release thread."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### topo_affinity"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"`topo_affinity` is designed for A2, Atlas 300 inference products, and\n"
+"other non-A3 device types. A2 and Atlas 300 inference products expose\n"
+"**meaningful NPU-to-CPU affinity information**, so the allocator starts from NPU\n"
+"topology affinity when it is available and then avoids overlap for shared\n"
+"affinity groups."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. Build candidate NPUs from all logical NPUs:\n"
+"   - always include running NPUs\n"
+"   - include non-running NPUs only when their affinity overlaps this process's allowed cpuset\n"
+"2. For each candidate NPU, intersect topo affinity with `allowed_cpus`.\n"
+"3. If the intersection is empty for a candidate, binding fails for this rank.\n"
+"4. If the affinity CPUs are all on one NUMA node, extend the pool with CPUs from the next NUMA node, constrained by `allowed_cpus`.\n"
+"5. Group NPUs with identical extended pools and split each shared pool evenly across that group.\n"
+"6. Keep only running NPUs in the final `npu_cpu_pool`."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"The non-running candidate step is intentional. It prevents two independent\n"
+"single-card workers from selecting the same CPU range when their visible NPUs\n"
+"share the same topology affinity."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Role Split"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Role | CPUs |\n"
+"| --- | --- |\n"
+"| SQ/CQ IRQ | `pool[0]`, `pool[1]` |\n"
+"| Main worker process and subthreads | `pool[2:-2]` |\n"
+"| ACL thread | `pool[-2]` |\n"
+"| Release thread | `pool[-1]` |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Role | CPUs |\n"
+"| --- | --- |\n"
+"| Main worker process and subthreads | `pool[:-2]` |\n"
+"| ACL thread | `pool[-2]` |\n"
+"| Release thread | `pool[-1]` |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"If a final pool has fewer CPUs than the selected role split requires, binding\n"
+"fails for this rank and the worker logs a warning from the caller. The minimum\n"
+"is 5 CPUs per NPU for devices with IRQ binding, and 3 CPUs per NPU for Ascend 950."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Conditional Host Tuning"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"After CPU affinity is applied, CPU binding can also apply two host-side tuning\n"
+"steps when the environment supports them:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Memory migration uses `migratepages` to move the worker process's existing\n"
+"  pages to the selected NUMA node. This keeps the worker closer to the memory it\n"
+"  reads and reduces remote-NUMA memory read latency.\n"
+"- IRQ binding places NPU IRQ handling on the CPUs reserved for the corresponding\n"
+"  NPU when `/proc/irq` is writable and IRQ files can be resolved.\n"
+"  Ascend 950 skips this step and gives those CPUs to the main worker instead."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"These are conditional parts of CPU binding, not separate feature switches. If a\n"
+"host prerequisite is missing, that step is skipped while CPU thread binding\n"
+"still proceeds. Missing `migratepages` can still leave pages on remote NUMA\n"
+"nodes, so **latency or throughput may regress compared with a full CPU binding\n"
+"setup.**"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Examples"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### A3 inference server with 640 CPUs and 16 NPUs"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `allowed_cpus = [0..639]`\n"
+"- `total_logic_npus = 16`\n"
+"- `running_npu_list = [0..15]`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `base = 640 // 16 = 40`\n"
+"- `extra = 0`\n"
+"- Worker `i` driving logical NPU `i` receives CPU slice\n"
+"  `[i * 40 .. i * 40 + 39]`."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Worker | Logical NPU | CPU pool | IRQ CPUs | Main CPUs | ACL CPU | Release CPU |\n"
+"| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n"
+"| 0 | 0 | 0-39 | 0-1 | 2-37 | 38 | 39 |\n"
+"| 1 | 1 | 40-79 | 40-41 | 42-77 | 78 | 79 |\n"
+"| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |\n"
+"| 15 | 15 | 600-639 | 600-601 | 602-637 | 638 | 639 |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"This layout remains deterministic even when different worker processes share\n"
+"the same cpuset, because slicing is based on the global logical NPU ID."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### A2 topo_affinity with hidden same-affinity NPUs"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- NPU0 affinity: 144-167\n"
+"- NPU2 affinity: 144-167\n"
+"- Process A sees only NPU0\n"
+"- Process B sees only NPU2\n"
+"- Both processes have `allowed_cpus = [144..191]`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"The allocator includes the hidden same-affinity NPU as a candidate in each\n"
+"process, splits the shared extended pool, and then keeps only the visible NPU in\n"
+"the final pool."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Process | Visible NPU | Final CPU pool |\n"
+"| --- | --- | --- |\n"
+"| A | 0 | 144-167 |\n"
+"| B | 2 | 168-191 |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Logs"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Limitations"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- CPU binding runs only on ARM. It is skipped on x86_64.\n"
+"- Each final NPU pool must have enough CPUs for its role split: at least 5 CPUs\n"
+"  for devices with IRQ binding, and at least 3 CPUs for Ascend 950.\n"
+"- `global_slice` is deterministic and provides CPU/NUMA isolation when the\n"
+"  cpuset is NUMA-aligned, but it cannot guarantee NUMA-local pools when CPU\n"
+"  numbering or cpuset layout crosses NUMA boundaries.\n"
+"- `topo_affinity` depends on usable output from `npu-smi info -t topo`.\n"
+"- IRQ binding requires writable `/proc/irq` and resolvable PCI/IRQ information.\n"
+"  Ascend 950 skips IRQ binding even when `/proc/irq` is writable, and does not reserve\n"
+"  IRQ CPUs in its role split.\n"
+"- Memory migration requires `migratepages`; otherwise only memory migration is\n"
+"  skipped. CPU affinity still applies, but performance may degrade because\n"
+"  existing pages are not moved to the target NUMA node and may be read through\n"
+"  higher-latency remote NUMA access.\n"
+"- If an exception escapes the binding flow, `NPUWorker` logs a warning and skips CPU binding for that rank."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## References"
+msgstr ""

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/disaggregated_prefill.md:10
 msgid ""
 "**Optimizing TPOT** Without the disaggregated-prefill strategy, prefill "
 "tasks are inserted during decoding, which results in inefficiencies and "
 "delays. Disaggregated-prefill solves this by allowing for better control "
-"over the system's **TPOT**. By managing chunked prefill tasks "
-"effectively, the system avoids the challenge of determining the optimal "
-"chunk size and provides more reliable control over the time taken for "
-"generating output tokens."
+"over the system's **TPOT**. By managing chunked prefill tasks effectively, "
+"the system avoids the challenge of determining the optimal chunk size and "
+"provides more reliable control over the time taken for generating output "
+"tokens."
 msgstr ""
 "**优化 TPOT**\n"
-"若不采用预填充-解码分离策略，预填充任务会插入解码过程中，导致效率低下和延迟。预填充-解码分离通过更好地控制系统 **TPOT** "
-"来解决此问题。通过有效管理分块预填充任务，系统避免了确定最优分块大小的难题，并能更可靠地控制输出 Token 的生成时间。"
+"若不采用预填充-解码分离策略，预填充任务会插入解码过程中，导致效率低下和延迟。预填充-解码分离通过更好地控制系统 **TPOT** 来解决此问题。通过有效管理分块预填充任务，系统避免了确定最优分块大小的难题，并能更可靠地控制输出 Token 的生成时间。"

+
+msgid "# Disaggregated-prefill"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Why disaggregated-prefill?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. **Adjusting Parallel Strategy and Instance Count for P and D Nodes**  \n"
+"   Using the disaggregated-prefill strategy, this feature allows the system to flexibly adjust the parallelization strategy (e.g., data parallelism (dp), tensor parallelism (tp), and expert parallelism (ep)) and the instance count for both P (Prefiller) and D (Decoder) nodes. This leads to better system performance tuning, particularly for **TTFT** and **TPOT**."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"2. **Optimizing TPOT**\n"
+"   Without the disaggregated-prefill strategy, prefill tasks are inserted during decoding, which results in inefficiencies and delays. Disaggregated-prefill solves this by allowing for better control over the system's **TPOT**. By managing chunked prefill tasks effectively, the system avoids the challenge of determining the optimal chunk size and provides more reliable control over the time taken for generating output tokens."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## How It Works"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 1. Design Approach"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 2. Implementation Design"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Our design diagram is shown below, illustrating the pull and push schemes respectively.\n"
+"![alt text](../../assets/disaggregated_prefill_pull.png)\n"
+"![alt text](../../assets/disaggregated_prefill_push.png)"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### Mooncake Connector"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. The request is sent to the Proxy's `_handle_completions` endpoint.\n"
+"2. The Proxy calls `select_prefiller` to choose a P node and forwards the request, configuring `kv_transfer_params` with `do_remote_decode=True`, `max_completion_tokens=1`, and `min_tokens=1`.\n"
+"3. After the P node's scheduler finishes prefill, `update_from_output` invokes the schedule connector's `request_finished` to defer KV cache release, constructs `kv_transfer_params` with `do_remote_prefill=True`, and returns to the Proxy.\n"
+"4. The Proxy calls `select_decoder` to choose a D node and forwards the request.\n"
+"5. On the D node, the scheduler marks the request as `RequestStatus.WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KVS`, pre-allocates KV cache, calls `kv_connector_no_forward` to pull the remote KV cache, then notifies the P node to release KV cache and proceeds with decoding to return the result."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### Mooncake Layerwise Connector"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. The request is sent to the Proxy's `_handle_completions` endpoint.\n"
+"2. The Proxy calls `select_decoder` to choose a D node and forwards the request, configuring `kv_transfer_params` with `do_remote_prefill=True` and setting the `metaserver` endpoint.\n"
+"3. On the D node, the scheduler uses `kv_transfer_params` to mark the request as `RequestStatus.WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KVS`, pre-allocates KV cache, then calls `kv_connector_no_forward` to send a request to the metaserver and waits for the KV cache transfer to complete.\n"
+"4. The Proxy's `metaserver` endpoint receives the request, calls `select_prefiller` to choose a P node, and forwards it with `kv_transfer_params` set to `do_remote_decode=True`, `max_completion_tokens=1`, and `min_tokens=1`.\n"
+"5. During processing, the P node's scheduler pushes KV cache layer-wise; once all layers pushing is complete, it releases the request and notifies the D node to begin decoding.\n"
+"6. The D node performs decoding and returns the result."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 3. Interface Design"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- **MooncakeConnector**: Base class that provides core interfaces.\n"
+"- **MooncakeConnectorScheduler**: Interface for scheduling the connectors within the engine core, responsible for managing KV cache transfer requirements and completion.\n"
+"- **MooncakeConnectorWorker**: Interface for managing KV cache registration and transfer in worker processes."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 4. Specifications Design"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Feature                       |      Status    |\n"
+"|-------------------------------|----------------|\n"
+"| A2                            | 🟢 Functional  |\n"
+"| A3                            | 🟢 Functional  |\n"
+"| equal TP configuration        | 🟢 Functional  |\n"
+"| unequal TP configuration      | 🟢 Functional  |\n"
+"| MLA                           | 🟢 Functional  |\n"
+"| GQA                           | 🟢 Functional  |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- 🟢 Functional: Fully operational, with ongoing optimizations.\n"
+"- 🔵 Experimental: Experimental support, interfaces and functions may change.\n"
+"- 🚧 WIP: Under active development, will be supported soon.\n"
+"- 🟡 Planned: Scheduled for future implementation (some may have open PRs/RFCs).\n"
+"- 🔴 NO plan/Deprecated: No plan or deprecated by vLLM."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## DFX Analysis"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 1. Config Parameter Validation"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 2. Port Conflict Detection"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 3. PD Ratio Validation"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Limitations"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Heterogeneous P and D nodes are not supported, for example, running P "
+"nodes on A2 and D nodes on A3."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- In non-symmetric TP configurations, only cases where the P nodes have a "
+"higher TP degree than the D nodes and the P TP count is an integer multiple "
+"of the D TP count are supported (i.e., P_tp > D_tp and P_tp % D_tp = 0)."
+msgstr ""

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/dynamic_chunked_pipeline_parallel.md:9
 msgid ""
-"In Pipeline Parallelism (PP) + Chunked Prefill scenarios, long sequences "
-"are split into fixed-size chunks that pass through the pipeline "
-"sequentially. Due to the O(n²) computational complexity of Self-"
-"Attention, **chunks of the same size take increasingly longer to process "
-"as the prefix sequence grows**:"
-msgstr "在流水线并行 (PP) + 分块预填充场景中，长序列被分割成固定大小的块，依次通过流水线。由于自注意力的 O(n²) 计算复杂度，**随着前缀序列增长，相同大小的块处理时间会越来越长**："
+"In Pipeline Parallelism (PP) + Chunked Prefill scenarios, long sequences are"

 msgid ""

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/dynamic_chunked_pipeline_parallel.md:158
-msgid "[Tutorial](../../tutorials/features/dynamic_chunked_pipeline_parallel.md)"
+msgid ""
+"[Tutorial](../../tutorials/features/dynamic_chunked_pipeline_parallel.md)"
 msgstr "[教程](../../tutorials/features/dynamic_chunked_pipeline_parallel.md)"
+
+msgid "# Dynamic Chunked Pipeline Parallel (CPP)"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Background"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Problem Statement"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Solution Overview"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Design"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Quadratic Latency Model"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "$$f(l) = a \\cdot l^2 + b \\cdot l + c$$"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- $a \\cdot l^2$: Attention overhead (quadratic)\n"
+"- $b \\cdot l$: Linear operations (FFN, projection)\n"
+"- $c$: Fixed overhead (kernel launch)"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Startup Phase: Profiling"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. **Sampling**: Uniformly sample 64 different chunk sizes from `base_chunk_size` down to near 0\n"
+"2. **Execution**: Perform real model forward passes for each chunk size and precisely measure latency (milliseconds)\n"
+"3. **Fitting**: Fit the quadratic model using least squares\n"
+"4. **Target Setting**: Calculate target per-chunk latency based on `base_chunk_size`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Runtime Phase: Dynamic Prediction"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "$$f(L + x) - f(L) = T$$"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "$$a \\cdot x^2 + (2aL + b) \\cdot x - T = 0$$"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "$$x = \\frac{-(2aL + b) + \\sqrt{(2aL + b)^2 + 4aT}}{2a}$$"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. **Smoothing**: Blend predicted chunk size with `base_chunk_size` using `smooth_factor`\n"
+"2. **Alignment**: Round down to multiple of `page_size` (minimum 64)\n"
+"3. **Constraints**: Not exceeding `max_model_len - history_len` and `max_num_scheduled_tokens`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Online Calibration"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "$$f(C, H) = a \\cdot C(C+H) + b \\cdot (C+H) + c$$"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Architecture"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Key Components"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Component | Location | Responsibility |\n"
+"|-----------|----------|---------------|\n"
+"| **ChunkSizePredictor** | `vllm_ascend/core/profiling_chunk_predictor.py` | Quadratic model fitting and prediction |\n"
+"| **ProfilingChunkManager** | `vllm_ascend/core/profiling_chunk_predictor.py` | Manage profiling workflow and predictor |\n"
+"| **Scheduler** | `vllm_ascend/core/scheduler_profiling_chunk.py` | Integrate CPP scheduling |\n"
+"| **EngineCore** | `vllm_ascend/patch/platform/patch_profiling_chunk.py` | Startup profiling, record execution time |\n"
+"| **NPUWorker** | `vllm_ascend/worker/worker.py` | Execute real forward pass profiling |\n"
+"| **NPUModelRunner** | `vllm_ascend/worker/model_runner_v1.py` | `profile_cpp=True` mode |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Workflow"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Comparison with SGLang"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Feature | SGLang Dynamic Chunking | Dynamic Chunked Pipeline Parallel |\n"
+"|---------|------------------------|-----------------------------------|\n"
+"| Profiling method | Preset quadratic function | Real forward pass profiling at startup |\n"
+"| Model fitting | $f(l) = a \\cdot l^2 + b \\cdot l + c$ | Same + online calibration $f(C,H)$ |\n"
+"| Online updates | None | History-based fitting |\n"
+"| Accuracy | May deviate on different hardware | Adapts to actual hardware performance |\n"
+"| Startup cost | None | ~64 forward passes (tens of seconds) |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Constraints"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- **Pipeline Parallelism Required**: Must set `--pipeline-parallel-size > 1`\n"
+"- **Chunked Prefill Required**: Must enable `--enable-chunked-prefill`\n"
+"- **Incompatible with Balance Scheduling**: Cannot enable `VLLM_ASCEND_BALANCE_SCHEDULING`\n"
+"- **Startup Overhead**: Profiling phase adds tens of seconds to initialization\n"
+"- **Memory**: No additional runtime memory overhead; profiling reuses existing dummy_run mechanism"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## References"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- [SGLang Dynamic Chunking Blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2026-01-15-chunked-pipeline/)\n"
+"- [User Guide](../../user_guide/feature_guide/dynamic_chunk_pipeline_parallel.md)\n"
+"- [Tutorial](../../tutorials/features/dynamic_chunked_pipeline_parallel.md)"
+msgstr ""

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/eplb_swift_balancer.md:5
 msgid ""
 "When using Expert Parallelism (EP), different experts are assigned to "
-"different NPUs. Given that the load of various experts may vary depending"
-" on the current workload, it is crucial to maintain balanced loads across"
-" different NPUs. We adopt a redundant experts strategy by duplicating "
-"heavily-loaded experts. Then, we heuristically pack these duplicated "
-"experts onto NPUs to ensure load balancing across them. Moreover, thanks "
-"to the group-limited expert routing used in MoE models, we also attempt "
-"to place experts of the same group on the same node to reduce inter-node "
-"data traffic, whenever possible."
+"different NPUs. Given that the load of various experts may vary depending on"
+" the current workload, it is crucial to maintain balanced loads across "
+"different NPUs. We adopt a redundant experts strategy by duplicating "
+"heavily-loaded experts. Then, we heuristically pack these duplicated experts"
+" onto NPUs to ensure load balancing across them. Moreover, thanks to the "
+"group-limited expert routing used in MoE models, we also attempt to place "
+"experts of the same group on the same node to reduce inter-node data "
+"traffic, whenever possible."
 msgstr ""
 "当使用专家并行（EP）时，不同专家被分配到不同 NPU 上。由于不同专家的负载可能随当前工作负载而变化，因此保持各 NPU "
 "之间的负载均衡至关重要。我们采用冗余专家策略，通过复制负载较重的专家来实现。然后，我们通过启发式方法将这些复制的专家打包到 NPU "
-"上，以确保它们之间的负载均衡。此外，借助 MoE "
-"模型中使用的组限制专家路由，我们尽可能尝试将同一组的专家放置在同一个节点上，以减少节点间的数据流量。"
+"上，以确保它们之间的负载均衡。此外，借助 MoE 模型中使用的组限制专家路由，我们尽可能尝试将同一组的专家放置在同一个节点上，以减少节点间的数据流量。"
 
 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/eplb_swift_balancer.md:7
 msgid ""
-"To facilitate reproduction and deployment, vLLM Ascend supports the "
-"deployed EP load balancing algorithm in `vllm_ascend/eplb/core/policy`. "
-"The algorithm computes a balanced expert replication and placement plan "
-"based on the estimated expert loads. Note that the exact method for "
-"predicting expert loads is outside the scope of this repository. A common"
-" method is to use a moving average of historical statistics."
+"To facilitate reproduction and deployment, vLLM Ascend supports the deployed"
+" EP load balancing algorithm in `vllm_ascend/eplb/core/policy`. The "
+"algorithm computes a balanced expert replication and placement plan based on"
+" the estimated expert loads. Note that the exact method for predicting "
+"expert loads is outside the scope of this repository. A common method is to "
+"use a moving average of historical statistics."
 msgstr ""
 "为了便于复现和部署，vLLM Ascend 在 `vllm_ascend/eplb/core/policy` 中支持已部署的 EP "
 "负载均衡算法。该算法根据估计的专家负载计算均衡的专家复制和放置方案。注意，预测专家负载的具体方法不在本仓库范围内。一种常见的方法是使用历史统计数据的移动平均值。"

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/eplb_swift_balancer.md:90
 msgid ""
-"When the number of server nodes evenly divides the number of expert "
-"groups, we use the hierarchical load balancing policy to leverage group-"
-"limited expert routing. We first pack the expert groups onto nodes "
-"evenly, ensuring balanced loads across different nodes. Then, we "
-"replicate the experts within each node. Finally, we pack the replicated "
-"experts onto individual NPUs to ensure load balancing across them. The "
-"hierarchical load balancing policy can be used in the prefilling stage "
-"with a smaller expert-parallel size."
+"When the number of server nodes evenly divides the number of expert groups, "
+"we use the hierarchical load balancing policy to leverage group-limited "
+"expert routing. We first pack the expert groups onto nodes evenly, ensuring "
+"balanced loads across different nodes. Then, we replicate the experts within"
+" each node. Finally, we pack the replicated experts onto individual NPUs to "
+"ensure load balancing across them. The hierarchical load balancing policy "
+"can be used in the prefilling stage with a smaller expert-parallel size."
 msgstr ""
 "当服务器节点数量能整除专家组数量时，我们使用分层负载均衡策略来利用组限制专家路由。我们首先将专家组均匀地打包到节点上，确保不同节点之间的负载均衡。然后，在每个节点内复制专家。最后，将复制的专家打包到各个"
 " NPU 上，以确保它们之间的负载均衡。分层负载均衡策略可在预填充阶段使用，此时专家并行规模较小。"
@@ -219,8 +217,8 @@ msgstr "全局负载均衡"

+"In other cases, we use the global load balancing policy, which replicates "
+"experts globally regardless of expert groups, and packs the replicated "
 "experts onto individual NPUs. This policy can be adopted in the decoding "
 "stage with a larger expert-parallel size."

+
+msgid "# Expert Parallelism Load Balancer (EPLB)"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Why We Need EPLB?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## How to Use EPLB?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## How It Works?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"**1. Adaptor Module**  \n"
+"*Handles registration and adaptation for different MoE model types*"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `vllm_adaptor.py`  \n"
+"  Implementation supporting Qwen3-MoE and DeepSeek models, standardizing parameter handling for policy algorithms"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"**2. Core Module**  \n"
+"*Implements core algorithms, updates, and asynchronous processing*"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- **Policy Submodule**  \n"
+"  *Load balancing algorithms with factory pattern instantiation*\n"
+"    - `policy_abstract.py`  \n"
+"    Abstract class for load balancing strategy interfaces\n"
+"    - `policy_default_eplb.py`  \n"
+"    Default implementation of open-source EPLB paper algorithm\n"
+"    - `policy_swift_balancer.py`  \n"
+"    Enhanced version optimizing expert swaps for low-bandwidth devices (e.g., A2)\n"
+"    - `policy_flashlb.py`  \n"
+"    Threshold-based adjustment reducing operational costs through layer-wise fluctuation detection\n"
+"    - `policy_random.py`  \n"
+"    Random policy for basic testing\n"
+"    - `policy_factory.py`  \n"
+"    Strategy factory for automatic algorithm instantiation"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `eplb_device_transfer_loader.py`  \n"
+"  Manages expert table/weight transmission and updates\n"
+"- `eplb_utils.py`  \n"
+"  Utilities for expert table initialization and mapping\n"
+"- `eplb_worker.py`  \n"
+"  Asynchronous algorithm orchestration and result processing"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `eplb_updator.py`  \n"
+"  Central coordinator for load balancing during inference workflows\n"
+"- `utils.py`  \n"
+"  General utilities for EPLB interface registration"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. Maintained original structure while improving technical clarity\n"
+"2. Standardized terminology\n"
+"3. Enhanced algorithm differentiation through concise descriptors\n"
+"4. Improved scoping through hierarchical presentation\n"
+"5. Preserved file/class relationships while optimizing readability"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Default Algorithm"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### Hierarchical Load Balancing"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### Global Load Balancing"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Add a New EPLB Policy"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. Inherit the `EplbPolicy` abstract class of `policy_abstract.py`  and override the `rebalance_experts` interface, ensuring consistent input parameters `current_expert_table`, `expert_workload` and return types `newplacement`.\n"
+"For example:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"2. To add a new EPLB algorithm, include the policy type and its "
+"corresponding implementation class in the `PolicyFactory` of "
+"`policy_factory.py`."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Add a New MoE Model"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. **Adapter File Modification**  \n"
+"   - Inherit or modify `vllm_ascend/eplb/adaptor/vllm_adaptor.py`\n"
+"   - Add processing logic for key parameters:\n"
+"     - `num_dense_layers`\n"
+"     - `global_expert_num`\n"
+"     - `num_roe_layers`\n"
+"   - Ensure parameter synchronization in the `model_register` function."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"2. **MoE Feature Integration**  \n"
+"   - Extend `vllm_ascend/eplb/utils.py` with MoE-specific methods  \n"
+"   - Implement required functionality for expert routing or weight management"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"3. **Registration Logic Update**  \n"
+"   - Add patch logic within the `model_register` function  \n"
+"   - Maintain backward compatibility with existing model types"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"4. **Validation & Testing**  \n"
+"   - Verify parameter consistency across layers  \n"
+"   - Test cross-device communication for expert tables  \n"
+"   - Benchmark against baseline implementations (e.g., Qwen3-MoE)"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Preserve existing interface contracts in abstract classes  \n"
+"- Use decorators for non-intrusive patch integration  \n"
+"- Leverage `eplb_utils.py` for shared expert mapping operations"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## DFX"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Parameter Validation"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### Integer Parameters"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### File Path"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Function Specifications"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### Initialization Function"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### General Functions"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Consistency"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### Expert Map"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"The expert map must be globally unique during initialization and update. In a multi-node scenario during initialization, distributed communication should be used to verify the consistency of expert maps across each rank. If they are inconsistent, the user should be notified of which ranks have inconsistent maps.\n"
+"During the update process, if only a few layers or the expert table of a certain rank has been changed, the updated expert table must be synchronized with the EPLB's context to ensure global consistency."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### Expert Weight"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Limitations"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Before using EPLB, start the script and add `export DYNAMIC_EPLB=\"true\"`.\n"
+"Before performing load data collection (or performance data collection), start the script and add `export EXPERT_MAP_RECORD=\"true\"`."
+msgstr ""

 msgid ""

-msgstr "通过复用 PyTorch 社区的 TORCH_COMPILE_DEBUG 环境变量，当设置 TORCH_COMPILE_DEBUG=1 时，将输出整个过程中的 FX 图。"
\ No newline at end of file
+"community, when TORCH_COMPILE_DEBUG=1 is set, it will output the FX graphs "
+"throughout the entire process."
+msgstr ""
+"通过复用 PyTorch 社区的 TORCH_COMPILE_DEBUG 环境变量，当设置 TORCH_COMPILE_DEBUG=1 "
+"时，将输出整个过程中的 FX 图。"
+
+msgid "# Npugraph_ex"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## How Does It Work?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Default FX Graph Optimization"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### FX Graph pass"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- For the intermediate nodes of the model, replace the non-in-place operators contained in the nodes with in-place operators to reduce memory movement during computation and improve performance.\n"
+"- For the original input parameters of the model, if they include in-place operators, Dynamo's Functionalize process will replace the in-place operators with a form of non-in-place operators + copy operators. npugraph_ex will reverse this process, restoring the in-place operators and reducing memory movement."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### FX fusion pass"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Custom fusion pass"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"|Parameter Name| Input/Output |Explanation|Is necessary|\n"
+"|--|--------------|---|-------|\n"
+"|search_fn|Input|This function is the operator combination or calculation logic that you want to recognize in the FX graph, such as the operator combination that needs to be fused|Yes|\n"
+"|replace_fn|Input|When the combination corresponding to search_fn is found in the target graph, this function's computation logic will replace the original subgraph to achieve operator fusion or optimization.|Yes|\n"
+"|example_inputs|Input|Example input tensors used to track search_fn and replace_fn. The shape and dtype of the input should match the actual scenario.|Yes|\n"
+"|trace_fn|Input|By default, only the forward computation graph is tracked, which is suitable for optimization during the inference phase; if training scenarios need to be supported, a function that supports backward tracking can be provided.|No|\n"
+"|extra_check|Input|Find the extra verification function after operator fusion. The function's input parameter must be a Match object from torch._inductor.pattern_matcher, and it is used for further custom checks on the matching result, such as checking whether the fused operators are on the same stream, checking the device type, checking the input shapes, and so on.|No|\n"
+"|search_fn_pattern|Input|A custom pattern object is generally unnecessary to provide. Its definition follows the rules of the native PyTorch MultiOutputPattern object. After passing this parameter, search_fn will no longer be used to match operator combinations; instead, this parameter will be used directly as the matching rule.|No|"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### DFX"
+msgstr ""
