 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/patch.md:75
 msgid ""
-"In V1 Engine, vLLM starts three kinds of processes: Main process, "
-"EngineCore process and Worker process. Now vLLM Ascend can only patch the"
-" code in Main process and Worker process by default. If you want to patch"
-" the code running in EngineCore process, you should patch EngineCore "
-"process entirely during setup. Find the entire code in "
-"`vllm.v1.engine.core`. Please override `EngineCoreProc` and "
-"`DPEngineCoreProc` entirely."
+"In V1 Engine, vLLM starts three kinds of processes: Main process, EngineCore"
+" process and Worker process. Now vLLM Ascend can only patch the code in Main"
+" process and Worker process by default. If you want to patch the code "
+"running in EngineCore process, you should patch EngineCore process entirely "
+"during setup. Find the entire code in `vllm.v1.engine.core`. Please override"
+" `EngineCoreProc` and `DPEngineCoreProc` entirely."
 msgstr ""
-"在 V1 引擎中，vLLM 启动三种进程：主进程、EngineCore 进程和 Worker 进程。目前 vLLM Ascend "
-"默认只能修补主进程和 Worker 进程中的代码。如果你想修补 EngineCore 进程中运行的代码，你需要在设置期间完全修补 EngineCore "
-"进程。完整代码位于 `vllm.v1.engine.core`。请完全重写 `EngineCoreProc` 和 `DPEngineCoreProc`。"
+"在 V1 引擎中，vLLM 启动三种进程：主进程、EngineCore 进程和 Worker 进程。目前 vLLM Ascend 默认只能修补主进程和 "
+"Worker 进程中的代码。如果你想修补 EngineCore 进程中运行的代码，你需要在设置期间完全修补 EngineCore 进程。完整代码位于 "
+"`vllm.v1.engine.core`。请完全重写 `EngineCoreProc` 和 `DPEngineCoreProc`。"
 
 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/patch.md:76
 msgid ""
 "If you are running edited vLLM code, the version of vLLM may be changed "
-"automatically. For example, if you run the edited vLLM based on v0.9.n, "
-"the version of vLLM may be changed to v0.9.nxxx. In this case, the patch "
-"for v0.9.n in vLLM Ascend would not work as expected, because vLLM Ascend"
-" can't distinguish the version of the vLLM you're using. In this case, "
-"you can set the environment variable `VLLM_VERSION` to specify the "
-"version of the vLLM you're using, and then the patch for that version "
-"(e.g., v0.9.n) should work."
+"automatically. For example, if you run the edited vLLM based on v0.9.n, the "
+"version of vLLM may be changed to v0.9.nxxx. In this case, the patch for "
+"v0.9.n in vLLM Ascend would not work as expected, because vLLM Ascend can't "
+"distinguish the version of the vLLM you're using. In this case, you can set "
+"the environment variable `VLLM_VERSION` to specify the version of the vLLM "
+"you're using, and then the patch for that version (e.g., v0.9.n) should "
+"work."
 msgstr ""
 "如果你运行的是编辑过的 vLLM 代码，vLLM 的版本可能会自动更改。例如，如果你基于 v0.9.n 运行编辑后的 vLLM，vLLM "
-"的版本可能会变为 v0.9.nxxx。在这种情况下，vLLM Ascend 中针对 v0.9.n 的补丁将无法按预期工作，因为 vLLM "
-"Ascend 无法区分你正在使用的 vLLM 版本。此时，你可以设置环境变量 `VLLM_VERSION` 来指定你正在使用的 vLLM "
-"版本，然后针对该版本（例如 v0.9.n）的补丁就应该能正常工作了。"
\ No newline at end of file
+"的版本可能会变为 v0.9.nxxx。在这种情况下，vLLM Ascend 中针对 v0.9.n 的补丁将无法按预期工作，因为 vLLM Ascend "
+"无法区分你正在使用的 vLLM 版本。此时，你可以设置环境变量 `VLLM_VERSION` 来指定你正在使用的 vLLM 版本，然后针对该版本（例如 "
+"v0.9.n）的补丁就应该能正常工作了。"
+
+msgid "# Patch in vLLM Ascend"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Principle"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. Less is more. Please do not patch unless it's the only way currently.\n"
+"2. Once a patch is added, it's required to describe the future plan for removing the patch.\n"
+"3. Anytime, cleaning the patch code is welcome."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## How it works"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- **platform**: The patch code in this directory is for patching the code in vLLM main process. It's called by `vllm_ascend/platform::NPUPlatform::pre_register_and_update` very early when vLLM is initialized.\n"
+"    - For online mode, vLLM process calls the platform patch in `vllm/vllm/engine/arg_utils.py::AsyncEngineArgs.add_cli_args` when parsing the cli args.\n"
+"    - For offline mode, vLLM process calls the platform patch in `vllm/vllm/engine/arg_utils.py::EngineArgs.create_engine_config` when parsing the input parameters.\n"
+"- **worker**: The patch code in this directory is for patching the code in vLLM worker process. It's called by `vllm_ascend/worker/worker::NPUWorker::__init__` when the vLLM worker process is initialized.\n"
+"    - For both online and offline mode, vLLM engine core process calls the worker patch in `vllm/vllm/worker/worker_base.py::WorkerWrapperBase.init_worker` when initializing the worker process."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## How to write a patch"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. Decide which version of vLLM we should patch. For example, after analysis, here we want to patch both `0.10.0` and `main` of vLLM.\n"
+"2. Decide which process we should patch. For example, here `distributed` belongs to the vLLM main process, so we should patch `platform`.\n"
+"3. Create the patch file in the right folder. The file should be named as `patch_{module_name}.py`. The example here is `vllm_ascend/patch/platform/patch_distributed.py`.\n"
+"4. Write your patch code in the new file. Here is an example:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"5. Import the patch file in `__init__.py`. In this example, add `import vllm_ascend.patch.platform.patch_distributed` into `vllm_ascend/patch/platform/__init__.py`.\n"
+"6. Add the description of the patch in `vllm_ascend/patch/__init__.py`. The description format is as follows:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"7. Add the Unit Test and E2E Test. Any newly added code in vLLM Ascend "
+"should contain the Unit Test and E2E Test as well. You can find more details"
+" in [test guide](../contribution/testing.md)"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Limitations"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. In V1 Engine, vLLM starts three kinds of processes: Main process, EngineCore process and Worker process. Now vLLM Ascend can only patch the code in Main process and Worker process by default. If you want to patch the code running in EngineCore process, you should patch EngineCore process entirely during setup. Find the entire code in `vllm.v1.engine.core`. Please override `EngineCoreProc` and `DPEngineCoreProc` entirely.\n"
+"2. If you are running edited vLLM code, the version of vLLM may be changed automatically. For example, if you run the edited vLLM based on v0.9.n, the version of vLLM may be changed to v0.9.nxxx. In this case, the patch for v0.9.n in vLLM Ascend would not work as expected, because vLLM Ascend can't distinguish the version of the vLLM you're using. In this case, you can set the environment variable `VLLM_VERSION` to specify the version of the vLLM you're using, and then the patch for that version (e.g., v0.9.n) should work."
+msgstr ""

-"scaling factors on-the-fly for each token/activation tensor with higher "
-"precision."
+"**Static vs Dynamic:** Static quantization uses pre-computed scaling factors"
+" with better performance, while dynamic quantization computes scaling "
+"factors on-the-fly for each token/activation tensor with higher precision."
 msgstr "**静态 vs 动态：** 静态量化使用预计算的缩放因子，性能更好；动态量化为每个 token/激活张量实时计算缩放因子，精度更高。"
 
 #: ../../source/developer_guide/Design_Documents/quantization.md:114
 msgid ""
-"**Granularity:** Refers to the scope of scaling factor computation (e.g.,"
-" per-tensor, per-channel, per-group)."
+"**Granularity:** Refers to the scope of scaling factor computation (e.g., "
+"per-tensor, per-channel, per-group)."
 msgstr "**粒度：** 指缩放因子计算的范围（例如 per-tensor、per-channel、per-group）。"
+
+msgid "# Quantization Adaptation Guide"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Quantization Feature Introduction"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Quantization Inference Process"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Quantization Algorithm Adaptation"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- **Step 1: Algorithm Design**. Define the algorithm ID (e.g., `W4A8_DYNAMIC`), determine supported layers (linear, moe, attention), and design the quantization scheme (static/dynamic, pertensor/perchannel/pergroup).\n"
+"- **Step 2: Registration**. Use the `@register_scheme` decorator in `vllm_ascend/quantization/methods/registry.py` to register your quantization scheme class."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- **Step 3: Implementation**. Create an algorithm implementation file, such as `vllm_ascend/quantization/methods/w4a8.py`, and implement the method class and logic.\n"
+"- **Step 4: Testing**. Use your algorithm to generate quantization configurations and verify correctness and performance on target models and hardware."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Quantized Model Adaptation"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- The original model has been successfully adapted in `vLLM Ascend`.\n"
+"- **Fused Module Mapping**: Add the model's `model_type` to `packed_modules_model_mapping` in `vllm_ascend/quantization/modelslim_config.py` (e.g., `qkv_proj`, `gate_up_proj`, `experts`) to ensure sharding consistency and correct loading."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- All quantization algorithms used by the quantized model have been "
+"integrated into the `quantization` module."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Currently Supported Quantization Algorithms"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Algorithm                | Weight | Activation | Weight Granularity | Activation Granularity | Type    | Description                                                                                                                                                        |\n"
+"| ------------------------ | ------ | ---------- | ------------------ | ---------------------- | ------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |\n"
+"| `W4A16`                  | INT4   | FP16/BF16  | Per-Group          | Per-Tensor             | Static  | 4-bit weight quantization with 16-bit activation precision, specifically designed for MoE model expert layers, supporting int32 format weight packing              |\n"
+"| `W8A16`                  | INT8   | FP16/BF16  | Per-Channel        | Per-Tensor             | Static  | 8-bit weight quantization with 16-bit activation precision, balancing accuracy and performance, suitable for linear layers                                         |\n"
+"| `W8A8`                   | INT8   | INT8       | Per-Channel        | Per-Tensor             | Static  | Static activation quantization, suitable for scenarios requiring high precision                                                                                    |\n"
+"| `W8A8_DYNAMIC`           | INT8   | INT8       | Per-Channel        | Per-Token              | Dynamic | Dynamic activation quantization with per-token scaling factor calculation                                                                                          |\n"
+"| `W4A8_DYNAMIC`           | INT4   | INT8       | Per-Group          | Per-Token              | Dynamic | Supports both direct per-channel quantization to 4-bit and two-step quantization (per-channel to 8-bit then per-group to 4-bit)                                    |\n"
+"| `W4A4_FLATQUANT_DYNAMIC` | INT4   | INT4       | Per-Channel        | Per-Token              | Dynamic | Uses FlatQuant for activation distribution smoothing before 4-bit dynamic quantization, with additional matrix multiplications for precision preservation          |\n"
+"| `W8A8_MIX`               | INT8   | INT8       | Per-Channel        | Per-Tensor/Token       | Mixed   | We support two deployment modes: PD Colocation (dynamic quantization for both P and D) and PD Disaggregation (dynamic-quant P and static-quant D) |"
+msgstr ""

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/contribution/doc_writing.md:467
 msgid ""
 "If a converter should render something other than shell, set "
-"`GeneratedScript.language` accordingly so Sphinx can highlight the "
-"generated literal block correctly."
-msgstr "如果转换器应渲染shell以外的内容，请相应设置`GeneratedScript.language`，以便Sphinx能正确高亮生成的文字块。"
+"`GeneratedScript.language` accordingly so Sphinx can highlight the generated"
+" literal block correctly."
+msgstr ""
+"如果转换器应渲染shell以外的内容，请相应设置`GeneratedScript.language`，以便Sphinx能正确高亮生成的文字块。"
+
+msgid "# Doc writing guide"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Guide to Writing Model Tutorial Doc"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Testable doc code block generation (``model-code``)"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- For **documentation authors**: how to insert testable command blocks into docs\n"
+"- For **developers**: how to add a new converter"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| converter_tag | Renders | YAML source |\n"
+"| --- | --- | --- |\n"
+"| `single_node` | A single node's env exports + `vllm serve` script | `test_cases[case_index]` |\n"
+"| `multi_node` | One host's env exports + `vllm serve` script | `deployment[host_index]` |\n"
+"| `external_dp_template` | One external-DP node's env exports + `vllm serve` command | `templates[host_index]` |\n"
+"| `external_dp_launch` | One `launch_online_dp.py` line per node | `config[]` |\n"
+"| `external_dp_proxy` | The load-balance proxy launch command | `config[]` + `routing` |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### For authors: add a block"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "!!! warning"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"By default, the generator scans only `.md` files under `docs/source/tutorials/models/` and produces artifacts.\n"
+"    If you put ``model-code`` blocks in other directories, Sphinx builds will not automatically generate the corresponding scripts."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Option | Required | Description |\n"
+"| --- | --- | --- |\n"
+"| `block_name` | Yes | Block name; must be unique within the current document |\n"
+"| `converter_tag` | Yes | Selects one of the built-in converters |\n"
+"| `test_case_path` | Yes | Repository-relative YAML path that stays within the repo; the file must exist |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Use the body of the block to add shell wrapper lines such as `set -eux`. Always\n"
+"place the `{{ generated }}` placeholder where the converter output should be\n"
+"inserted."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### converter_tag: `single_node`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"`single_node` reads one item from `test_cases`. The optional `case_index`\n"
+"metadata selects the item; when omitted, it defaults to `0`."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Only the fields read by this converter are expanded below. Other test metadata\n"
+"can be left in the YAML and is ignored by this converter."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"`envs` is rendered as `export` lines. `SERVER_PORT: DEFAULT_PORT` is resolved\n"
+"to the default single-node port `8000`. `model` becomes `vllm serve <model>`,\n"
+"and `server_cmd` plus optional `server_cmd_extra` become command arguments.\n"
+"Both command fields can be either a shell string or a flat token list."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+":block_name: qwen3_8b_single_node\n"
+":converter_tag: single_node\n"
+":test_case_path: tests/e2e/nightly/single_node/models/configs/your_model.yaml\n"
+":case_index: 0"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"set -eux\n"
+"{{ generated }}"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### converter_tag: `multi_node`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"`multi_node` reads one item from `deployment`. The required `host_index`\n"
+"metadata selects which host to render."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"`server_cmd` must be a complete command starting with `vllm serve <model>`.\n"
+"It can be written as a shell string or a flat token list."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+":block_name: qwen3_235b_worker_1\n"
+":converter_tag: multi_node\n"
+":test_case_path: tests/e2e/nightly/multi_node/internal_dp/config/your_model.yaml\n"
+":host_index: 1"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### converter_tag: `external_dp_template`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"`external_dp_template` reads one item from `templates`. The required\n"
+"`host_index` metadata selects which template to render. The top-level `model`\n"
+"field is also required because the converter builds `vllm serve <model>`."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Known braced template variables are rewritten to the positional shell arguments\n"
+"that `run_dp_template.sh` receives from `launch_online_dp.py`:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Template variable | Rendered positional |\n"
+"| --- | --- |\n"
+"| `${VISIBLE_DEVICES}` | `$1` |\n"
+"| `${PORT}` | `$2` |\n"
+"| `${DP_SIZE}` | `$3` |\n"
+"| `${DP_RANK}` | `$4` |\n"
+"| `${DP_ADDRESS}` | `$5` |\n"
+"| `${DP_RPC_PORT}` | `$6` |\n"
+"| `${TP_SIZE}` | `$7` |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Unknown braced variables and unbraced shell references such as `$SERVER_PORT`\n"
+"are left unchanged."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+":block_name: glm_external_dp_template_node0\n"
+":converter_tag: external_dp_template\n"
+":test_case_path: tests/e2e/nightly/multi_node/external_dp/config/your_model.yaml\n"
+":host_index: 0"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"set -eux\n"
+"export HCCL_IF_IP=$local_ip\n"
+"export GLOO_SOCKET_IFNAME=$nic_name\n"
+"export TP_SOCKET_IFNAME=$nic_name\n"
+"export HCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME=$nic_name"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "{{ generated }}"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### converter_tag: `external_dp_launch`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"`external_dp_launch` reads the full `config` list and renders one\n"
+"`launch_online_dp.py` command per node. It does not take an index option."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+":block_name: glm_external_dp_launch\n"
+":converter_tag: external_dp_launch\n"
+":test_case_path: tests/e2e/nightly/multi_node/external_dp/config/your_model.yaml"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### converter_tag: `external_dp_proxy`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"`external_dp_proxy` reads `config` and `routing`. It renders the\n"
+"`load_balance_proxy_server_example.py` command for `routing.type:\n"
+"disaggregated_prefill`. It does not take an index option."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"`routing.groups.prefiller` and `routing.groups.decoder` contain indices into\n"
+"`config`. Each referenced node expands to `dp_size_local` host and port entries.\n"
+"The proxy itself is rendered on `${NODE_0_IP}:1999`."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+":block_name: glm_external_dp_proxy\n"
+":converter_tag: external_dp_proxy\n"
+":test_case_path: tests/e2e/nightly/multi_node/external_dp/config/your_model.yaml"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Local debugging and generation"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### Generate only (without building the full site)"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "!!! note"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### Build the site & preview locally"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### For developers: add a new converter"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"A converter turns one loaded YAML file plus one parsed `ModelCodeBlock` into a\n"
+"`GeneratedScript`. The current pipeline is:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. `BlockScanner` parses ``model-code`` fences and accepts only options listed\n"
+"   in `MODEL_CODE_OPTION_NAMES`.\n"
+"2. `YamlLoader` loads `test_case_path`.\n"
+"3. `get_converter()` looks up `block.converter_tag` from\n"
+"   `build_default_converters()`.\n"
+"4. The selected converter returns `GeneratedScript(content=..., language=\"shell\")`.\n"
+"5. `GeneratorService` replaces `{{ generated }}` in the block body, validates\n"
+"   that the final script is non-empty, and writes\n"
+"   `docs/_build/doc_codegen/<doc_stem>/<block_name>.sh`."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. In `tools/docs_codegen/converters.py`, add a `BaseConverter` subclass with a\n"
+"   unique `name`. That name is the value authors put in `:converter_tag:`.\n"
+"2. Implement `convert(self, loaded_yaml, *, block) -> GeneratedScript`. Use\n"
+"   `make_docs_codegen_error(..., block=block)` for user-facing validation\n"
+"   errors so the CLI and Sphinx output include document context.\n"
+"3. Reuse helpers from `tools/docs_codegen/utils.py`, such as\n"
+"   `require_mapping`, `require_mapping_list`, `require_scalar_mapping`,\n"
+"   `require_indexed_mapping`, `require_node_field`, `parse_command_tokens`,\n"
+"   `substitute_template_positionals`, and `render_cli_command`.\n"
+"4. Register the converter in `build_default_converters()`. If it is not\n"
+"   registered, `get_converter()` will reject the new `converter_tag`.\n"
+"5. If the converter needs new directive metadata, add the option name to\n"
+"   `MODEL_CODE_OPTION_NAMES` in `tools/docs_codegen/scanner.py` and to\n"
+"   `ModelCodeDirective.option_spec` in\n"
+"   `tools/docs_codegen/sphinx_extension.py`. Read the option with\n"
+"   `block.get_option(\"<option_name>\")`.\n"
+"6. Add or update tests in `tests/ut/tools/test_docs_codegen.py`. Cover the\n"
+"   successful render path, required option validation, YAML shape validation,\n"
+"   and any CLI/Sphinx scanner behavior affected by new metadata.\n"
+"7. Add a real ``model-code`` example in a model tutorial, preferably under\n"
+"   `docs/source/tutorials/models/`, and point it to an existing YAML file under\n"
+"   `tests/`.\n"
+"8. Validate with the CLI:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"If a converter should render something other than shell, set\n"
+"`GeneratedScript.language` accordingly so Sphinx can highlight the generated\n"
+"literal block correctly."
+msgstr ""

 #: ../../source/developer_guide/contribution/e2e_ci_test.md:153
 msgid ""
-"Only the PR author or write/admin collaborators can use the comment "
-"trigger. Ask a maintainer to post the `/e2e` comment instead."
+"Only the PR author or write/admin collaborators can use the comment trigger."
+" Ask a maintainer to post the `/e2e` comment instead."
 msgstr "只有 PR 作者或具有写入/管理员权限的协作者可以使用评论触发功能。请让维护者代为发布 `/e2e` 评论。"
 
+msgid "# E2E CI Test"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"This document explains how to trigger specific E2E tests against your PR code via a\n"
+"comment command, without running the full E2E test suite."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Background"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"The `E2E-Full` workflow (`pr_test.yaml`) normally runs the complete E2E test suite\n"
+"when a PR has `ready` label. This is expensive in CI resources\n"
+"and time."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Authorized users can trigger only the specific test files they care about by posting a\n"
+"`/e2e` comment on the PR, then adding the `ready` label."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## How to Trigger"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 1. Post a comment"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Each path must be a valid pytest path relative to the repository root.\n"
+"- Multiple paths can be listed in a single comment, separated by spaces.\n"
+"- A specific test case can be targeted using `::` notation."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Comment format | Effect |\n"
+"|---|---|\n"
+"| `/e2e tests/e2e/pull_request/one_card/test_foo.py` | Run one test file on one_card |\n"
+"| `/e2e tests/e2e/pull_request/two_card/test_bar.py` | Run one test file on two_card |\n"
+"| `/e2e path1 path2 path3` | Run multiple files, routed by path pattern |\n"
+"| `/e2e tests/e2e/pull_request/one_card/test_foo.py::test_case` | Run a specific test case |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 2. Add the label"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"After posting the comment, add the **`ready`** label to your PR.\n"
+"Adding the label is what actually **triggers** the workflow — at that point the workflow\n"
+"reads the existing comments to find the `/e2e` command."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "!!! note"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Only repository **Contributors** (Triage role) and **Maintainers** (Write role) can add\n"
+"    labels. If you do not have this permission, ask a maintainer to add the label for you.\n"
+"    You can find the list of maintainers and contributors by checking the\n"
+"    [CODEOWNERS](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/blob/main/.github/CODEOWNERS)\n"
+"    file."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "!!! warning"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"The comment must be posted **before** the label is added. If you add the label first,\n"
+"    the workflow will find no `/e2e` comment and will not trigger any per-test runs."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Additionally, only the **PR author** or collaborators with **write or admin** repository\n"
+"    access can trigger tests via comment. The workflow validates the commenter's permission\n"
+"    before proceeding."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 3. Wait for results"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"GitHub Actions will trigger the `E2E-Full` workflow. Only the hardware jobs matching\n"
+"the provided test paths will run, which saves CI resources."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Path Routing Rules"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"The workflow automatically routes each test path to the correct hardware runner based\n"
+"on path patterns:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Path pattern | Hardware | Runner |\n"
+"|---|---|---|\n"
+"| `two_card` in path | two_card A3 NPU | `linux-aarch64-a3-2` |\n"
+"| `four_card` in path | four_card A3 NPU | `linux-aarch64-a3-4` |\n"
+"| `_310p` in filename under one/two_card | Ascend 310P x1 | `linux-aarch64-310p-*` |\n"
+"| `_310p` in filename under four_card | Ascend 310P x4 | `linux-aarch64-310p-*` |\n"
+"| All other paths | one_card A2 NPU | `linux-aarch64-a2b3-1` |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"When paths from multiple categories are listed in a single comment, each category's\n"
+"tests run on its respective hardware in parallel."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Test Path Reference"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"310P tests use `_310p` subdirectories or `_310p.py` filename suffix under the\n"
+"corresponding card directory:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Comparison with Full E2E Suite"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Aspect | Full E2E suite | Per-test comment trigger |\n"
+"|---|---|---|\n"
+"| Trigger | `ready` labels | `/e2e` comment + `ready` label |\n"
+"| Scope | All E2E tests | Only specified test paths |\n"
+"| Who can trigger | Anyone who can add labels | PR author or write/admin collaborator |\n"
+"| Use case | Pre-merge validation | Iterative debugging of specific tests |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Examples"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Re-trigger after fixing an issue: just push a new commit. The `synchronize` event\n"
+"re-runs the workflow and picks up the existing `/e2e` comment automatically — no need\n"
+"to post a new comment."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Troubleshooting"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Make sure the `/e2e` comment was posted **before** the label was added.\n"
+"  If the label was added first, remove it and re-add it after posting the comment.\n"
+"- Check that the comment starts exactly with `/e2e` followed by at least one path,\n"
+"  with no leading spaces or extra characters before the slash.\n"
+"- To re-trigger after fixing an issue, simply push a new commit — the workflow will\n"
+"  reuse the existing `/e2e` comment automatically."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Check that the path includes the expected directory segment (`one_card`, `two_card`,\n"
+"  `four_card`, or `_310p`). Paths that do not match any of these patterns are routed to\n"
+"  the one_card runner by default."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Only the PR author or write/admin collaborators can use the comment trigger.\n"
+"  Ask a maintainer to post the `/e2e` comment instead."
+msgstr ""
+
 #~ msgid "`multicard/2-cards` in path"
 #~ msgstr "路径中包含 `multicard/2-cards`"

+msgid "# Contributing"
+msgstr ""
+

+
+msgid ""
+"It's recommended to set up a local development environment to build vllm-ascend and run tests\n"
+"before you submit a PR."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### Set up a development environment"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Theoretically, the vllm-ascend build is only supported on Linux because\n"
+"`vllm-ascend` dependency `torch_npu` only supports Linux."

+

+
+msgid "#### Run CI locally"

+

+
+msgid "### Testing locally"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## DCO and Signed-off-by"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## PR Title and Classification"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `[Attention]` for new features or optimization in attention.\n"
+"- `[Communicator]` for new features or optimization in communicators.\n"
+"- `[ModelRunner]` for new features or optimization in model runner.\n"
+"- `[Platform]` for new features or optimization in platform.\n"
+"- `[Worker]` for new features or optimization in worker.\n"
+"- `[Core]` for new features or optimization  in the core vllm-ascend logic (such as platform, attention, communicators, model runner)\n"
+"- `[Kernel]` for changes affecting compute kernels and ops.\n"
+"- `[BugFix]` for bug fixes.\n"
+"- `[Doc]` for documentation fixes and improvements.\n"
+"- `[Test]` for tests (such as unit tests).\n"
+"- `[CI]` for build or continuous integration improvements.\n"
+"- `[Misc]` for PRs that do not fit the above categories. Please use this sparingly."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "!!! note"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Others"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""

+msgstr ""

+"确认条目是格式良好的 YAML，位于 `.github/workflows/configs/nightly_config.yaml` 下。`name` "

+msgstr ""
+"如果第一个节点的日志不再足以提供有效的日志信息，请查看任务摘要以下载对应测试的日志归档，其中包含框架侧日志和每个节点的 plog 信息，结构如下："
+
+msgid "# Nightly CI Test"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"This document explains how to trigger nightly hardware CI tests against your own PR code\n"
+"on Ascend NPU hardware (A2/A3), without waiting for the scheduled nightly run."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Background"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"By default, nightly CI tests run on a fixed schedule using pre-built nightly images.\n"
+"Contributors can self-service trigger these tests directly against their PR changes\n"
+"by combining a GitHub label with a comment command."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## How to Trigger"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 1. Post a comment"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Post one of the following comments in the PR to specify which tests to run.\n"
+"The comment itself triggers the workflow — no label is required."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Comment | Effect |\n"
+"|---------|--------|\n"
+"| `/nightly` | Run **all** nightly tests |\n"
+"| `/nightly all` | Run **all** nightly tests (same as above) |\n"
+"| `/nightly test1 test2 ...` | Run only the **named** tests |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "!!! note"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Only repository **Contributors** (Triage role) and **Maintainers** (Write role) can\n"
+"    trigger the `/nightly` command. If you do not have this permission, ask a maintainer\n"
+"    to post the comment for you. You can find the list of maintainers and contributors in\n"
+"    the project's [Governance](../../community/governance.md) page or by checking the\n"
+"    [CODEOWNERS](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/blob/main/.github/CODEOWNERS)\n"
+"    file."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 2. Wait for results"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"GitHub Actions will trigger the `Nightly-A2` or `Nightly-A3` workflow. Only tests\n"
+"matching the filter will be dispatched, which saves hardware resources."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Differences Between PR and Scheduled Runs"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| | Scheduled / Manual Dispatch | PR-triggered |\n"
+"|---|----------------------------|---|\n"
+"| Trigger | Cron (daily) or `workflow_dispatch` | `/nightly` comment |\n"
+"| Code tested | Pre-built nightly image | Your PR's HEAD commit (source installed fresh) |\n"
+"| Test scope | All tests | Configurable via `/nightly <names>` |\n"
+"| vLLM + vllm-ascend | From image | Checked out and installed from source |\n"
+"| Test matrix | From main branch's matrix YAML | From PR branch's matrix YAML |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. Uninstalls any existing vllm packages in the container.\n"
+"2. Checks out the specific vllm version and your PR's vllm-ascend commit from source.\n"
+"3. Installs all dependencies from source.\n"
+"4. Installs the `aisbench` benchmark suite."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Test Matrix Data Source"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"The set of nightly test cases (their names, runners, test paths, model configs) is\n"
+"declared in a single data file:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"The file is organized as `a2:` and `a3:` top-level keys (one per SoC). Under each\n"
+"SoC, tests are grouped by execution shape (single-node, multi-node, double-node,\n"
+"multi-card, accuracy) and each group holds a `test_config` (or `nightly` / `pr_only`\n"
+"for accuracy) list whose entries carry a `name` plus the fields consumed by the\n"
+"downstream reusable workflows (`os`, `tests`, `config_file_path`, `size`, etc.)."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Both the `Nightly-A2` and `Nightly-A3` workflows dynamically read this file at run\n"
+"time — there is no hardcoded test matrix in the workflow YAMLs. The\n"
+"`/nightly <name>` slash command resolves names by walking the same file from the\n"
+"PR branch, so newly added entries can be exercised on a PR before they land on\n"
+"main."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Adding a New Nightly Test Case"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. Append an entry under the appropriate section in\n"
+"   `.github/workflows/configs/nightly_config.yaml`. Each entry needs at least:\n"
+"   - `name`: unique identifier used in `/nightly <name>` filters\n"
+"   - `os` (for single-node / multi-card pytest+yaml tests) or `runner` is inferred\n"
+"   - one of `tests:` (pytest directory) or `config_file_path:` (YAML-driven model config)\n"
+"   - `size` (multi-node / double-node only)\n"
+"2. Add the actual test files (pytest modules under `tests/e2e/nightly/...` or\n"
+"   YAML model configs in `tests/e2e/nightly/.../configs/`).\n"
+"3. Open a PR. Once CI is green, you can validate the new entry against real NPU\n"
+"   hardware **without** merging the PR — see *Examples* below."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Available Test Names"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"The test names you can pass to `/nightly` correspond to the `name` fields under\n"
+"the matching section in `.github/workflows/configs/nightly_config.yaml`. The\n"
+"tables below mirror the current contents of that file."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### A2 workflow (`.github/workflows/schedule_nightly_test_a2.yaml`)"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Test name | Description |\n"
+"|-----------|-------------|\n"
+"| `test_custom_op_multi_card` | Custom operator tests (multi card) |\n"
+"| `qwen3-vl-32b-instruct-w8a8` | Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct W8A8 |\n"
+"| `qwen3-32b-int8` | Qwen3-32B INT8 quantization |\n"
+"| `Qwen3.5-27B-w8a8-A2` | Qwen3.5-27B W8A8 |\n"
+"| `Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-w4a8-mtp` | Qwen3.5-397B-A17B W4A8 + MTP |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Test name | Description |\n"
+"|-----------|-------------|\n"
+"| `multi-node-qwen3-235b-dp` | Qwen3-235B-A22B, 2-node DP |\n"
+"| `multi-node-GLM-5.1-w8a8-A2` | GLM-5.1 W8A8, 2 nodes |\n"
+"| `multi-node-Kimi-K2.5-W4A8-A2` | Kimi-K2.5 W4A8, 2 nodes |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Test name | Description | Scope |\n"
+"|-----------|-------------|-------|\n"
+"| `accuracy-group-1` | Qwen3-VL-8B, Qwen3-8B, Qwen2-Audio-7B, etc. | nightly |\n"
+"| `accuracy-group-2` | ERNIE-4.5, Molmo-7B, Llama-3.2-3B, etc. | nightly |\n"
+"| `accuracy-group-3` | Qwen3-30B-A3B, Qwen3-VL-30B-A3B, etc. | nightly |\n"
+"| `accuracy-group-4` | Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B, Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B, etc. | nightly |\n"
+"| `pr-accuracy-group-1` | gemma-3-4b-it, internlm3-8b-instruct, etc. | pr_only |\n"
+"| `pr-accuracy-group-2` | Qwen2.5-Math-RM-72B, Hunyuan-A13B-Instruct | pr_only |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"The `pr-accuracy-group-*` entries only run on `/nightly` (PR-triggered) runs;\n"
+"`/nightly all` on the schedule skips them."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### A3 workflow (`.github/workflows/schedule_nightly_test_a3.yaml`)"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Test name | Description |\n"
+"|-----------|-------------|\n"
+"| `multi-node-deepseek-v3.2-W8A8-EP` | DeepSeek-V3.2-W8A8 with EP, 4-node |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Test name | Description |\n"
+"|-----------|-------------|\n"
+"| `multi-node-deepseek-r1-w8a8-longseq` | DeepSeek-R1-W8A8 long sequence, 2-node |\n"
+"| `multi-node-qwen3-dp` | Qwen3-235B-A22B, 2-node DP |\n"
+"| `multi-node-qwenw8a8-2node-eplb` | Qwen3-235B-W8A8 with EPLB, 2-node |\n"
+"| `multi-node-dpsk3.2-2node` | DeepSeek-V3.2-W8A8, 2-node |\n"
+"| `multi-node-qwenw8a8-2node-longseq` | Qwen3-235B-W8A8 long sequence, 2-node |\n"
+"| `multi-node-qwen-disagg-pd` | Qwen3-235B disaggregated PD, 2-node |\n"
+"| `multi-node-qwen-vl-disagg-pd` | Qwen3-VL-235B disaggregated PD, 2-node |\n"
+"| `multi-node-deepseek-v3.1` | DeepSeek-V3.1-BF16, 2-node |\n"
+"| `multi-node-deepseek-v3.2-W8A8-EP` | DeepSeek-V3.2-W8A8 with EP, 4-node |\n"
+"| `multi-node-glm-5.2` | GLM-5.1-W8A8, 2-node |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Test name | Description |\n"
+"|-----------|-------------|\n"
+"| `mtpx-deepseek-r1-0528-w8a8` | MTP-X + DeepSeek-R1-0528-W8A8 |\n"
+"| `deepseek-r1-0528-w8a8` | DeepSeek-R1-0528-W8A8 |\n"
+"| `kimi-k2-thinking` | Kimi-K2-Thinking |\n"
+"| `qwen3-vl-235b-a22b-instruct-w8a8` | Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Instruct-W8A8 |\n"
+"| `deepseek-r1-0528-w8a8-prefix-cache` | DeepSeek-R1-0528-W8A8 prefix cache |\n"
+"| `deepseek-v3-2-w8a8` | DeepSeek-V3.2-W8A8 |\n"
+"| `glm-4.7-w8a8` | GLM-4.7 W8A8 |\n"
+"| `kimi-k2.5` | Kimi-K2.5 |\n"
+"| `qwen3-235b-a22b-w8a8` | Qwen3-235B-A22B-W8A8 |\n"
+"| `Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-w8a8-mtp` | Qwen3.5-397B-A17B W8A8 + MTP |\n"
+"| `MiniMax-M2.5-w8a8-QuaRot-A3` | MiniMax-M2.5 W8A8 + QuaRot |\n"
+"| `Qwen3.5-27B-w8a8-A3` | Qwen3.5-27B W8A8 |\n"
+"| `Qwen3.5-122B-A10B-W8A8-A3` | Qwen3.5-122B-A10B W8A8 |\n"
+"| `DeepSeek-V4-Flash-W8A8-A3` | DeepSeek-V4-Flash W8A8 |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Test name | Description |\n"
+"|-----------|-------------|\n"
+"| `qwen3-30b-acc` | Qwen3-30B accuracy test |\n"
+"| `qwen3-30b-a3b-w8a8` | Qwen3-30B-A3B-W8A8 |\n"
+"| `qwen3-32b-int8` | Qwen3-32B-Int8 |\n"
+"| `qwen3-32b-int8-prefix-cache` | Qwen3-32B-Int8 prefix cache |\n"
+"| `Qwen3-30B-A3B-W4A8-llm-compressor` | Qwen3-30B-A3B W4A8 via llm-compressor |\n"
+"| `Qwen3-30B-QuaRot` | Qwen3-30B QuaRot + eagle3 |\n"
+"| `Qwen3-32B-QuaRot` | Qwen3-32B QuaRot + eagle3 |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "!!! warning"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"The A3 resource pool has a maximum concurrency of **5×16 NPUs**. Multi-node tests\n"
+"    run with `max-parallel: 2` to avoid resource exhaustion. Running `/nightly all` on\n"
+"    A3 will queue a large number of jobs — prefer targeting specific test names when\n"
+"    possible."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Examples"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Re-trigger after fixing an issue: just push a new commit. The `synchronize` event\n"
+"re-runs the workflow and picks up the existing `/nightly` comment automatically — no\n"
+"need to post a new comment."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Adding a New Test Case — Worked Example"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. Edit `.github/workflows/configs/nightly_config.yaml`, append under\n"
+"   `a2.single_node.test_config`:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "2. Commit the new pytest file (`test_my_new.py`) in the same PR."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "3. Trigger from the PR:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `pr_nightly_command.yml` reads your PR's `nightly_config.yaml` and resolves\n"
+"  `my-new-test` → dispatch A2 only.\n"
+"- `Nightly-A2` is dispatched at `main`, but `generate-a2-matrix` checks out your\n"
+"  PR commit and reads the new entry from the matrix.\n"
+"- `single-node-tests` runs one matrix job for `my-new-test`, with\n"
+"  `should_run=true`. The reusable workflow checks out your PR code (via\n"
+"  `vllm_ascend_ref`) and runs your pytest."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Troubleshooting"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Check that the comment starts exactly with `/nightly` with no leading spaces or\n"
+"  extra characters before the slash.\n"
+"- Confirm you have at least Triage permission on the repository; unauthorized\n"
+"  users' comments are ignored.\n"
+"- To re-trigger after fixing an issue, simply push a new commit — the workflow will\n"
+"  reuse the existing `/nightly` comment automatically."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Test names are case-sensitive and must match the `name` field in\n"
+"  `.github/workflows/configs/nightly_config.yaml` exactly (see the tables above).\n"
+"- For a PR-triggered run, the matrix is loaded from your PR's\n"
+"   `nightly_config.yaml`, not main. If a name isn't in your PR's file, it won't\n"
+"  be recognized and the dispatch will be skipped.\n"
+"- Check the `parse-trigger` job output in GitHub Actions for the resolved\n"
+"  `test_filter` value."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Confirm the workflow was triggered by `repository_dispatch` (slash command),\n"
+"  not bare `workflow_dispatch`. The `pr_nightly_command.yml` workflow is what\n"
+"  actually dispatches `schedule_nightly_test_a2.yaml` / `_a3.yaml` with\n"
+"  `vllm_ascend_ref` pointing at your PR SHA."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Confirm the entry is well-formed YAML under\n"
+"  `.github/workflows/configs/nightly_config.yaml`. The `name` field is required\n"
+"  and must be unique within the SoC's section.\n"
+"- The matrix is loaded from your PR branch, so make sure the file is committed\n"
+"  to the same branch the `/nightly` comment was posted on."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- For most issues, the stdout pop-up logs from GitHub actions are sufficient (this log always represents the logs from the first node).\n"
+"- If the logs from a first node are no longer sufficient to provide effective logging information, see the summary of your jobs to download log archive for the corresponding test, which includes the framework-side logs and plog information for each node, structured as follows:"
+msgstr ""

+
+msgid "### Unit tests"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- The test file path should be consistent with the source file and start with the `test_` prefix, such as: `vllm_ascend/worker/worker.py` --> `tests/ut/worker/test_worker.py`\n"
+"- The vLLM Ascend test uses unittest framework. See [the Python unittest documentation](https://docs.python.org/3/library/unittest.html#module-unittest) to understand how to write unit tests.\n"
+"- All unit tests can be run on CPUs, so you must mock the device-related functions on the host.\n"
+"- Example: [tests/ut/test_ascend_config.py](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/blob/main/tests/ut/test_ascend_config.py).\n"
+"- You can run the unit tests using `pytest`:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "=== \"Single-card\""
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "=== \"Multi-card\""
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### E2E test"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Although vllm-ascend CI provides E2E tests on Ascend CI (for example,\n"
+"[schedule_nightly_test_a2.yaml](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/blob/main/.github/workflows/schedule_nightly_test_a2.yaml), [schedule_nightly_test_a3.yaml](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/blob/main/.github/workflows/schedule_nightly_test_a3.yaml), [pr_test.yaml](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/blob/main/.github/workflows/pr_test.yaml)), you can run them locally."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### PR-triggered E2E test"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### Nightly-triggered E2E test"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### E2E test examples"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Offline test example: [`tests/e2e/pull_request/one_card/test_camem.py`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/blob/main/tests/e2e/pull_request/one_card/test_camem.py)\n"
+"- Online test example: [`tests/e2e/pull_request/two_card/aclgraph/test_single_request_aclgraph.py`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/blob/main/tests/e2e/pull_request/two_card/aclgraph/test_single_request_aclgraph.py)\n"
+"- Correctness test example: [`tests/e2e/pull_request/one_card/aclgraph/test_aclgraph_accuracy.py`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/blob/main/tests/e2e/pull_request/one_card/aclgraph/test_aclgraph_accuracy.py)"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. Fork the original model repo in modelscope. All the files in the repo except for weights are required.\n"
+"2. Set `num_hidden_layers` to the expected number of layers, e.g., `{\"num_hidden_layers\": 2,}`\n"
+"3. Copy the following python script as `generate_random_weight.py`. Set the relevant parameters `MODEL_LOCAL_PATH`, `DIST_DTYPE` and `DIST_MODEL_PATH` as needed:"
+msgstr ""
+

+

+"The doctest is a good way to make sure docs stay current and examples remain executable, which can be run locally as follows:"

+

+

+
+msgid "#### Configuration"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Update the file `benchmark/ais_bench/benchmark/configs/models/vllm_api/vllm_api_general_chat.py`.\n"
+"There are several arguments that you should update according to your environment."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `attr`: Identifier for the inference backend type, fixed as `service` (serving-based inference) or `local` (local model).\n"
+"- `type`: Used to select different backend API types.\n"
+"- `abbr`: Unique identifier for a local task, used to distinguish between multiple tasks.\n"
+"- `path`: Update to your model weight path.\n"
+"- `model`: Update to your model name in vLLM.\n"
+"- `host_ip` and `host_port`: Update to your vLLM server ip and port.\n"
+"- `max_out_len`: Note `max_out_len` + LLM input length should be less than `max_model_len`(config in your vllm server), `32768` will be suitable for most datasets.\n"
+"- `batch_size`: Update according to your dataset.\n"
+"- `temperature`: Update inference argument."
+msgstr ""
+

+msgid "# MSProbe Debugging Guide"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## 0. Background Concepts"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- **L0**: dumps tensors at the module level and generates `construct.json` so that visualization tools can rebuild the network structure. A model or submodule handle must be passed in.\n"
+"- **L1**: collects operator-level statistics only, which is suitable for lightweight troubleshooting.\n"
+"- **mix**: captures both structural information and operator statistics, which is useful when you need both graph reconstruction and numerical comparisons."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## 1. Prerequisites"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 1.1 Install `msprobe`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 1.2 Graph mode dump (optional)"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "1. Install `aclgraph_dump` from source code:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## 2. Collecting Data with `msprobe`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 2.1 Prepare the dump configuration content"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Pass the config object directly through `--additional-config.dump_config`.\n"
+"- Pass a config file path through `--additional-config.dump_config_path`."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"| Field       | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Required | Eager Mode | Graph Mode |\n"
+"|:-----------:|:-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|:--------:|:-------------:|:-------------:|\n"
+"| `task`      | Type of dump task. Common PyTorch values include `\"statistics\"` and `\"tensor\"`. A statistics task collects tensor statistics (mean, variance, max, min, etc.) while a tensor task captures arbitrary tensors. |    Yes   |      ✅       |      ✅       |\n"
+"| `dump_path` | Directory where dump results are stored. When omitted, `msprobe` uses its default path.                                                                                                                     |    No    |      ✅       |      ✅       |\n"
+"| `rank`      | Ranks to sample. An empty list collects every rank. For single-card tasks, you must set this field to `[]`.                                                                                                 |    No    |      ✅       |      ✅       |\n"
+"| `step`      | Token iteration(s) to sample. An empty list means every iteration.                                                                                                                                         |    No    |      ✅       |      ❌       |\n"
+"| `level`     | Dump level string (`\"L0\"`, `\"L1\"`, or `\"mix\"`). `L0` targets `nn.Module`, `L1` targets `torch.api`, and `mix` collects both.                                                                                 |    Yes   |      ✅       |      ✅       |\n"
+"| `async_dump`| Whether to enable asynchronous dump (supported for PyTorch `statistics`/`tensor` tasks). Defaults to `false`.                                                                                              |    No    |      ✅       |      ❌       |\n"
+"| `scope`     | Module range to sample. An empty list collects every module.                                                                                                                                                |    No    |      ✅       |      ❌       |\n"
+"| `dump_enable` | Dynamic switch for enabling/disabling dump in `PrecisionDebugger` during one running training/inference job. This allows turning dump on or off on demand in the same job.                             |    No    |      ✅       |      ❌       |\n"
+"| `list`      | Operator range to sample. An empty list collects every operator.                                                                                                                                            |    No    |      ✅       |      ✅       |"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `scope` (list[str]): In PyTorch PyNative scenarios this field restricts "
+"the dump range. Provide two module or API names that follow the tool's "
+"naming convention to lock a range; only data between the two names will be "
+"dumped. Examples:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `list` (list[str]): Custom operator list. Options include:\n"
+"    - Supply the full names of specific APIs in PyTorch pynative scenarios to only dump those APIs. Example: `\"list\": [\"Tensor.permute.1.forward\", \"Tensor.transpose.2.forward\", \"Torch.relu.3.forward\"]`.\n"
+"    - When `level=mix`, you can provide module names so that the dump expands to everything produced while the module is running. Example: `\"list\": [\"Module.module.language_model.encoder.layers.0.mlp.ParallelMlp.forward.0\"]`.\n"
+"    - Provide a substring such as `\"list\": [\"relu\"]` to dump every API whose name contains the substring. When `level=mix`, modules whose names contain the substring are also expanded."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Example configuration:\n"
+"eager mode:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## 3. Enable `msprobe` in vllm-ascend"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. Start vLLM and pass the dump config content through `--additional-"
+"config`:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## 4. Send requests and collect dumps"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "1. Send inference requests as usual, for example:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"2. Each request drives the sequence `msprobe: start -> forward -> stop -> "
+"step`. The runner invokes `step()` on every code path, so you always get a "
+"complete dataset even if inference returns early."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"3. Dump files are written into `dump_path`. They usually contain:\n"
+"   - Tensor files grouped by operator/module.\n"
+"   - `dump.json`, which records metadata such as dtype, shape, min/max, and `requires_grad`.\n"
+"   - `construct.json`, which is generated when `level` is `L0` or `mix` (required for visualization)."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Example directory layout:\n"
+"   eager mode:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `rank`: Device ID. Each card writes its data to the corresponding `rank{ID}` directory. In non-distributed scenarios the directory is simply named `rank`.\n"
+"   - `dump_tensor_data`: Tensor payloads that were collected.\n"
+"   - `dump.json`: Statistics for the forward data of each API or module, including names, dtype, shape, max, min, mean, L2 norm (square root of the L2 variance), and CRC-32 when `summary_mode=\"md5\"`. See [dump.json file description](#dumpjson-file-description) for details.\n"
+"   - `dump_error_info.log`: Present only when the dump tool encountered an error and records the failure log.\n"
+"   - `stack.json`: Call stacks for APIs/modules.\n"
+"   - `construct.json`: Hierarchical structure description. Empty when `level=L1`."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `dump.json`: Statistics for the forward data of each API or module, "
+"including names, dtype, shape, max, min, mean, L2 norm (square root of the "
+"L2 variance), and CRC-32 when `summary_mode=\"md5\"`. See [dump.json file "
+"description](#dumpjson-file-description) for details."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## 5. Analyze the results"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 5.1 Prerequisites"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 5.2 Visualization"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. Ensure dump data is visualization-ready:\n"
+"   - Dump level must be `L0` or `mix` so `construct.json` is non-empty.\n"
+"   - Each rank directory should contain `dump.json`, `stack.json`, and `construct.json`."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"2. Choose command mode:\n"
+"   - Single-graph build:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "- Graph comparison:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Common optional flags:\n"
+"     - `-oc` / `--overflow_check`: enable overflow marking\n"
+"     - `-fm` / `--fuzzy_match`: enable fuzzy matching for node mapping\n"
+"     - `-lm` / `--layer_mapping [mapping.yaml]`: cross-framework/layer mapping compare\n"
+"     - `-tensor_log`: print per-node compare log (tensor dump scenarios)\n"
+"     - `-progress_log`: print detailed progress log"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"3. Path granularity is auto-detected by `graph_visualize`:\n"
+"   - Single-rank: `.../step0/rank0`\n"
+"   - Multi-rank (batch): `.../step0`\n"
+"   - Multi-step (batch): dump root path containing `step*`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"4. Output files:\n"
+"   - Single-graph build: `build_{timestamp}.vis.db`\n"
+"   - Graph comparison: `compare_{timestamp}.vis.db`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "5. Launch TensorBoard with the output directory:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"6. In the visualization UI, inspect structure and numeric differences:\n"
+"   - Switch rank/step to locate unstable nodes quickly.\n"
+"   - Use search/filter to focus on target ops/modules.\n"
+"   - For compare mode, prioritize highlighted high-difference nodes and trace surrounding I/O/parameters."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## 6. Troubleshooting"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `RuntimeError: Please enforce eager mode`: Restart vLLM and add the `--enforce-eager` flag.\n"
+"- No dump files: Confirm that the JSON path is correct and every node has write permission. In distributed scenarios set `keep_all_ranks` so that every rank writes its own dump.\n"
+"- Dumps are too large: Start with a `statistics` task to locate abnormal tensors, then narrow the scope with `scope`/`list`/`tensor_list`, `filters`, `token_range`, etc."
+msgstr ""
+

+msgid "# Optimization and Tuning"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Preparation"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "!!! note"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "## Optimizations"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 1. Memory Allocator Optimization"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### 1.1. jemalloc"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### 1.2. Tcmalloc"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 2. `torch_npu` Optimization"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 3. CANN Optimization"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### 3.1. HCCL Optimization"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- `HCCL_INTRA_ROCE_ENABLE`: Use RDMA link instead of SDMA link between two 8Ps as the mesh interconnect link. Find more [details](https://www.hiascend.com/document/detail/zh/Pytorch/600/ptmoddevg/trainingmigrguide/performance_tuning_0044.html).\n"
+"- `HCCL_RDMA_TC`: Use this var to configure traffic class of RDMA NIC. Find more [details](https://www.hiascend.com/document/detail/zh/Pytorch/600/ptmoddevg/trainingmigrguide/performance_tuning_0045.html).\n"
+"- `HCCL_RDMA_SL`: Use this var to configure service level of RDMA NIC. Find more [details](https://www.hiascend.com/document/detail/zh/Pytorch/600/ptmoddevg/trainingmigrguide/performance_tuning_0046.html).\n"
+"- `HCCL_BUFFSIZE`: Use this var to control the cache size for sharing data between two NPUs. Find more [details](https://www.hiascend.com/document/detail/zh/Pytorch/600/ptmoddevg/trainingmigrguide/performance_tuning_0047.html)."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 4. Kernel Optimization"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Forces all CPU cores to run under the `performance` governor\n"
+"- Disables dynamic frequency scaling (e.g., `ondemand`, `powersave`)"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Keeps CPU cores at maximum frequency\n"
+"- Reduces latency jitter\n"
+"- Improves predictability for inference workloads"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### 4.2 Disable Swap Usage"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "- Minimizes the kernel’s tendency to swap memory pages to disk"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Prevents severe latency spikes caused by swapping\n"
+"- Improves stability for large in-memory models"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- For inference workloads, swap can introduce second-level latency\n"
+"- Recommended values are `0` or `1`"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### 4.3 Disable Automatic NUMA Balancing"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "- Disables the kernel’s automatic NUMA page migration mechanism"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Prevents background memory page migrations\n"
+"- Reduces unpredictable memory access latency\n"
+"- Improves performance stability on NUMA systems"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Multi-socket servers\n"
+"- Ascend / NPU deployments with explicit NUMA binding\n"
+"- Systems with manually managed CPU and memory affinity"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "#### 4.4 Increase Scheduler Migration Cost"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Increases the cost for the scheduler to migrate tasks between CPU cores"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Reduces frequent thread migration\n"
+"- Improves CPU cache locality\n"
+"- Lowers latency jitter for inference workloads"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- Unit: nanoseconds (ns)\n"
+"- Typical recommended range: 50000–100000\n"
+"- Higher values encourage threads to stay on the same CPU core"
+msgstr ""
+

+msgstr "" + +msgid "## Solution Comparison" +msgstr "" + +msgid "" +"| Feature | Ascend PyTorch Profiler | MS Service Profiler |\n" +"|:-----|:------------------------|:------------------|\n" +"| Installation Method | Built-in, no additional installation required | Requires building msserviceprofiler from source |\n" +"| Collection Granularity | PyTorch operator level | Service framework function level |\n" +"| Control Method | API request control | Configuration file control |\n" +"| Applicable Scenarios | Model operator performance analysis | Service framework workflow analysis |\n" +"| Data Format | ascend_pt format | Chrome Tracing + CSV |\n" +"| Main Advantage | Operator-level performance analysis | Service framework workflow visualization |\n" +"| Supported Collection Capabilities | PyTorch operator level | PyTorch operator level and Service framework function level |" +msgstr "" + +msgid "## Quick Selection Guide" +msgstr "" + +msgid "" +"- [**Model Operator Performance** → Use Ascend PyTorch Profiler](#ascend-pytorch-profiler)\n" +"- [**Service Framework Workflow** → Use MS Service Profiler](#ms-service-profiler)" +msgstr "" + +msgid "## MS Service Profiler" +msgstr "" + +msgid "### 0. Build from Source and Upgrade" +msgstr "" + +msgid "### 1. Preparation" +msgstr "" + +msgid "### 2. Enable Profiling" +msgstr "" + +msgid "### 3. Send Requests" +msgstr "" + +msgid "### 4. Parse Data" +msgstr "" + +msgid "### 5. View Results" +msgstr "" + +msgid "" +"- `chrome_tracing.json`: Chrome tracing format data, which can be opened in [MindStudio Insight](https://www.hiascend.com/document/detail/zh/mindstudio/830/GUI_baseddevelopmenttool/msascendinsightug/Insight_userguide_0002.html?framework=mindspore).\n" +"- `profiler.db`: Performance data in database format.\n" +"- `request.csv`: Request-related data.\n" +"- `kvcache.csv`: KV Cache-related data.\n" +"- `batch.csv`: Batch scheduling-related data." +msgstr "" + +msgid "### 6. Appendix related to MS Service Profiler" +msgstr "" + +msgid "(profiling-configuration-file)=" +msgstr "" + +msgid "#### 6.1 Profiling Configuration File" +msgstr "" + +msgid "##### File Format" +msgstr "" + +msgid "" +"| Parameter | Description | Required |\n" +"|:------:|:------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|:-----:|\n" +"| enable | Switch for profiling:
0: disable
1: enable
Default: 0 | Yes |\n" +"| prof_dir | Directory to store collected performance data.
Default: `${HOME}/.ms_server_profiler` | No |\n" +"| profiler_level | Data collection level. Default is \"INFO\" (normal level). | No |\n" +"| acl_task_time | Switch to collect operator dispatch latency and execution latency. Values:
0: off. Default; 0 or any invalid value means off.
1: on. When enabled, calls `aclprofCreateConfig` with `ACL_PROF_TASK_TIME_L0`.
2: on. MSPTI-based dump. When enabled, set before starting the service: `export LD_PRELOAD={INSTALL_DIR}/lib64/libmspti.so`, where `{INSTALL_DIR}` is the CANN installation root (e.g. `/usr/local/Ascend/cann` for a typical root install).
3: on. Torch Profiler–based dump. | No |\n" +"| acl_prof_task_time_level | Profiling level and duration. Values:
L0: collect operator dispatch and execution latency only; lower overhead (no operator basic info).
L1: collect AscendCL interface performance (host–device and inter-device sync/async memory copy latencies), plus operator dispatch, execution, and basic info for comprehensive analysis.
`{time}`: optional duration segment; integer 1–999, unit seconds.
If unset, defaults to L0 until program exit; invalid values fall back to defaults.
Level and duration can be combined, e.g., `\"acl_prof_task_time_level\": \"L1;10\"`.
**Note:** When Torch Profiler is used (`acl_task_time` set to `3`), `{time}` duration is not supported. | No |\n" +"| timelimit | Profiling duration for the service. The process stops automatically after this time. Range: integer 0–7200, unit: seconds. Default 0 means unlimited. Recommend at least 120 s; shorter runs may lack data for parsed outputs and trigger warnings. | No |\n" +"| domain | Limit profiling to the specified domains to reduce data volume. String, separated by semicolons, case-sensitive, e.g., \"Request; KVCache\".
Empty means all available domains.
Available domains: Request, KVCache, ModelExecute, BatchSchedule, Communication.
Note: If the selected domains are incomplete, analysis output may show warnings due to missing data. See [Reference Table 1](https://www.hiascend.com/document/detail/zh/canncommercial/850/devaids/Profiling/mindieprofiling_0010.html). | No |\n" +"| torch_prof_stack | Collect operator call stacks (framework and CPU operators). Values: `false` (default, off), `true` (on). Requires `acl_task_time` set to `3`. **Note:** Enabling this configuration introduces additional performance overhead. | No |\n" +"| torch_prof_step_num | Torch Profiler step limit. Integer ≥ 0. Default `0` means collect all steps.
Requires `acl_task_time` set to `3`. | No |\n" +"| profiler_step_num | Step limit for operator and service framework profiling. Integer ≥ 0.
`0` or invalid values stop the entire service profiling process.
The number of steps actually recorded depends on `modelRunnerExec` events. | No |" +msgstr "" + +msgid "##### Example Configuration" +msgstr "" + +msgid "(symbols-configuration-file)=" +msgstr "" + +msgid "#### 6.2 Symbols Configuration File" +msgstr "" + +msgid "##### File Name and Loading" +msgstr "" + +msgid "" +"- Default load " +"path:`~/.config/vllm_ascend/service_profiling_symbols.MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH.yaml`(" +" According to the installed version of vllm )" +msgstr "" + +msgid "##### Configuration file updates" +msgstr "" + +msgid "##### Field Descriptions" +msgstr "" + +msgid "" +"| Field | Description | Example |\n" +"|:-----:|:-----|:-----|\n" +"| symbol | Python import path + attribute chain | `\"vllm.v1.core.kv_cache_manager:KVCacheManager.free\"` |\n" +"| handler | Handler type | `\"timer\"` (default) or `\"pkg.mod:func\"` (custom) |\n" +"| domain | Domain tag | `\"KVCache\"`, `\"ModelExecute\"` |\n" +"| name | Event name | `\"EngineCoreExecute\"` |\n" +"| min_version | Minimum supported vLLM version | `\"0.9.1\"` |\n" +"| max_version | Maximum supported vLLM version | `\"0.11.0\"` |\n" +"| attributes | Custom attribute collection | Only supported for `\"timer\"` handler. See the section below |" +msgstr "" + +msgid "##### Configuration Examples" +msgstr "" + +msgid "- Example 1: Custom handler" +msgstr "" + +msgid "- Example 2: Default timer" +msgstr "" + +msgid "- Example 3: Version constraint" +msgstr "" + +msgid "##### Custom Attribute Collection" +msgstr "" + +msgid "###### Basic Syntax" +msgstr "" + +msgid "" +"- Argument access: use the parameter name directly, e.g., `input_ids`\n" +"- Return value access: use the `return` keyword\n" +"- Pipeline operations: use `|` to chain multiple operations\n" +"- Attribute access: use `attr` to access object attributes" +msgstr "" + +msgid "###### Example" +msgstr "" + +msgid "###### Expression Notes" +msgstr "" + +msgid "" +"1. `len(input_ids)`: get the length of parameter `input_ids`.\n" +"2. `len(return) | str`: get the length of the return value and convert to string (equivalent to `str(len(return))`).\n" +"3. `return[0] | attr input_ids | len`: get the length of the `input_ids` attribute of the first element in the return value." +msgstr "" + +msgid "###### Supported Expression Types" +msgstr "" + +msgid "" +"- Basic operations: `len()`, `str()`, `int()`, `float()`\n" +"- Index access: `return[0]`, `return['key']`\n" +"- Attribute access: `return | attr attr_name`\n" +"- Pipeline composition: chain operations with `|`" +msgstr "" + +msgid "###### Advanced Examples" +msgstr "" + +msgid "##### Custom Handler" +msgstr ""

 msgid ""
-"Enabling NPU graph mode for DeepSeek may trigger an error. This is "

-"16 queries per KV—a value outside the supported range. Support for NPU "
-"graph mode on DeepSeek-V2-Lite will be added in a future update."
+"Enabling NPU graph mode for DeepSeek may trigger an error. This is because "
+"when both MLA (Multi-Head Latent Attention) and NPU graph mode are active, "
+"the number of queries per KV head must be 32, 64, or 128. However, "
+"DeepSeek-V2-Lite has only 16 attention heads, which results in 16 queries "
+"per KV—a value outside the supported range. Support for NPU graph mode on "
+"DeepSeek-V2-Lite will be added in a future update."
 msgstr ""
-"为 DeepSeek 启用 NPU 图模式可能会报错。这是因为当同时启用 MLA（多头潜在注意力）和 NPU 图模式时，每个 KV "
-"头的查询数必须为 32、64 或 128。然而，DeepSeek-V2-Lite 仅有 16 个注意力头，导致每个 KV 仅有 16 "
-"个查询，超出了支持范围。对 DeepSeek-V2-Lite 的 NPU 图模式支持将在未来更新中添加。"
+"为 DeepSeek 启用 NPU 图模式可能会报错。这是因为当同时启用 MLA（多头潜在注意力）和 NPU 图模式时，每个 KV 头的查询数必须为 "
+"32、64 或 128。然而，DeepSeek-V2-Lite 仅有 16 个注意力头，导致每个 KV 仅有 16 个查询，超出了支持范围。对 "
+"DeepSeek-V2-Lite 的 NPU 图模式支持将在未来更新中添加。"

+"guidance is intentionally narrow: it is only added when capture fails with "

 #: ../../source/faqs.md:286
 msgid ""
-"When testing a vLLM server, one may find that TPOT increases as "

-"However, sometimes TPOT may increase dramatically (10 to 100ms for "
-"example) as concurrency grows. This is possibly caused by "
+"When testing a vLLM server, one may find that TPOT increases as concurrency "
+"increases (for example, TPOT increases by 0.5 ~ 1ms when concurrency "
+"increases by 4). This phenomenon is normal in most cases. However, sometimes"
+" TPOT may increase dramatically (10 to 100ms for example) as concurrency "
+"grows. This is possibly caused by "
 "[**PREEMPTION**](https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/configuration/optimization/#preemption)"
-" in vLLM. Generally, when your server hits KV cache limits, vLLM tries to"
-" free KV cache of requests to ensure sufficient space for other requests,"
-" which is called preemption in vLLM. When a request is preempted, the "
-"default behavior is to recompute the KV cache of this request again in "
-"the future, which is why the performance might drop significantly. There "
-"are several ways to verify this:"
+" in vLLM. Generally, when your server hits KV cache limits, vLLM tries to "
+"free KV cache of requests to ensure sufficient space for other requests, "
+"which is called preemption in vLLM. When a request is preempted, the default"
+" behavior is to recompute the KV cache of this request again in the future, "
+"which is why the performance might drop significantly. There are several "
+"ways to verify this:"
 msgstr ""
 "在测试 vLLM 服务器时，你可能会发现 TPOT 随着并发数增加而增加（例如，并发数增加 4 时，TPOT 增加 0.5 ~ 1 "
-"毫秒）。在大多数情况下，这种现象是正常的。然而，有时 TPOT 可能会随着并发数增长而急剧上升（例如增加 10 到 100 毫秒）。这可能是由 "
-"vLLM 中的 "
+"毫秒）。在大多数情况下，这种现象是正常的。然而，有时 TPOT 可能会随着并发数增长而急剧上升（例如增加 10 到 100 毫秒）。这可能是由 vLLM"
+" 中的 "
 "[**抢占**](https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/configuration/optimization/#preemption)"
 " 引起的。通常，当你的服务器达到 KV 缓存限制时，vLLM 会尝试释放某些请求的 KV 缓存，以确保为其他请求提供足够的空间，这在 vLLM "
 "中称为抢占。当一个请求被抢占时，默认行为是在未来重新计算该请求的 KV 缓存，这就是性能可能显著下降的原因。有几种方法可以验证这一点："

 "suggested in "
 "[**PREEMPTION**](https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/configuration/optimization/#preemption),"
-" the core strategy is to increase available KV cache. For example, one "
-"can increase `--gpu-memory-utilization` or decrease `--max-num-seqs` && "
-"`--max-num-batched-tokens`."

-" 中所建议的，核心策略是增加可用的 KV 缓存。例如，可以增加 `--gpu-memory-utilization` 或减少 `--max-"

 #: ../../source/faqs.md:294
 msgid "23. How do I choose between single-node and multi-node deployment?"
 msgstr "23.如何在单节点和多节点部署之间选择？"
 
 #: ../../source/faqs.md:296
 msgid ""
-"Single-node deployment is recommended when the model fits within the "
-"memory of a single node's NPUs. For models like Qwen3-32B (BF16), which "
-"requires 4 × 64G cards, multi-NPU within a single node (TP) is "
-"sufficient. Multi-node deployment is only needed when the total NPU count"
-" exceeds a single node's capacity."
+"Single-node deployment is recommended when the model fits within the memory "
+"of a single node's NPUs. For models like Qwen3-32B (BF16), which requires 4 "
+"× 64G cards, multi-NPU within a single node (TP) is sufficient. Multi-node "
+"deployment is only needed when the total NPU count exceeds a single node's "
+"capacity."
 msgstr ""
-"当模型适配在单个节点的 NPU 内存中时，建议使用单节点部署。对于像 Qwen3-32B (BF16) 这样需要 4 × 64G 卡的模型，单个节点内的多 NPU (TP) 就足够了。只有当总 NPU 数量超过单个节点容量时，才需要多节点部署。"
+"当模型适配在单个节点的 NPU 内存中时，建议使用单节点部署。对于像 Qwen3-32B (BF16) 这样需要 4 × 64G "
+"卡的模型，单个节点内的多 NPU (TP) 就足够了。只有当总 NPU 数量超过单个节点容量时，才需要多节点部署。"

 msgid "24. What quantization method should I use?"
 msgstr "24.我应该使用哪种量化方法？"
 
 #: ../../source/faqs.md:300
 msgid ""
-"**BF16**: Best accuracy, highest memory footprint. Use for accuracy-"
-"critical applications or when memory is sufficient."

+" applications or when memory is sufficient."

 #: ../../source/faqs.md:301
 msgid ""
 "**W8A8**: Good balance of accuracy and memory reduction. Use for large "
 "models (e.g., 32B) on memory-constrained hardware."
-msgstr ""
-"**W8A8**：精度和内存缩减的良好平衡。适用于内存受限硬件上的大型模型（例如 32B）。"

 #: ../../source/faqs.md:304
 msgid "25. When should I enable FlashComm_v1?"
 msgstr "25.何时应该启用 FlashComm_v1？"
 
 #: ../../source/faqs.md:306
 msgid ""
-"Enable FlashComm_v1 (`VLLM_ASCEND_ENABLE_FLASHCOMM1=1`) when using Tensor"
-" Parallelism (TP ≥ 2) with high concurrency. It is threshold-protected "
-"and will not activate in low-concurrency scenarios where it could degrade"
-" performance."
+"Enable FlashComm_v1 (`VLLM_ASCEND_ENABLE_FLASHCOMM1=1`) when using Tensor "
+"Parallelism (TP ≥ 2) with high concurrency. It is threshold-protected and "
+"will not activate in low-concurrency scenarios where it could degrade "
+"performance."
 msgstr ""
-"在使用张量并行 (TP ≥ 2) 且高并发时启用 FlashComm_v1 (`VLLM_ASCEND_ENABLE_FLASHCOMM1=1`)。它具有阈值保护，在可能降低性能的低并发场景下不会激活。"
+"在使用张量并行 (TP ≥ 2) 且高并发时启用 FlashComm_v1 "
+"(`VLLM_ASCEND_ENABLE_FLASHCOMM1=1`)。它具有阈值保护，在可能降低性能的低并发场景下不会激活。"
 
 #: ../../source/faqs.md:308
 msgid "26. What is the difference between FIA and PA operators for attention?"
 msgstr "26.FIA 和 PA 注意力算子有什么区别？"
 
 #: ../../source/faqs.md:310
 msgid ""
-"FIA (Flash Attention) is the default attention operator in vLLM-Ascend. "
-"In some batch-size settings (particularly medium concurrency), FIA may "
-"exhibit suboptimal performance. The PA (Page Attention) operator can be "
-"manually enabled via `pa_shape_list` in `--additional-config`. When the "
-"runtime batch size matches a value in `pa_shape_list`, the framework "
-"switches to PA. This is a temporary tuning knob — future FIA "
-"optimizations will make this parameter obsolete."
+"FIA (Flash Attention) is the default attention operator in vLLM-Ascend. In "
+"some batch-size settings (particularly medium concurrency), FIA may exhibit "
+"suboptimal performance. The PA (Page Attention) operator can be manually "
+"enabled via `pa_shape_list` in `--additional-config`. When the runtime batch"
+" size matches a value in `pa_shape_list`, the framework switches to PA. This"
+" is a temporary tuning knob — future FIA optimizations will make this "
+"parameter obsolete."
+msgstr ""
+"FIA (Flash Attention) 是 vLLM-Ascend 中的默认注意力算子。在某些批次大小设置下（特别是中等并发），FIA "
+"可能表现出次优性能。PA (Page Attention) 算子可以通过 `--additional-config` 中的 "
+"`pa_shape_list` 手动启用。当运行时批次大小与 `pa_shape_list` 中的值匹配时，框架会切换到 "
+"PA。这是一个临时的调优参数——未来的 FIA 优化将使此参数过时。"
+

+msgid "### 10. How is vllm-ascend tested?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- **Functional test**: We added CI, including part of vllm's native unit "
+"tests and vllm-ascend's own unit tests. In vllm-ascend's tests, we test "
+"basic functionalities, popular model availability, and [supported "
+"features](https://docs.vllm.ai/projects/ascend/en/latest/user_guide/support_matrix/supported_features.html)"
+" through E2E test."
+msgstr ""

+msgid ""
+"- **Performance test**: We provide [benchmark](https://github.com/vllm-"
+"project/vllm-ascend/tree/main/benchmarks) tools for E2E performance "
+"benchmark, which can be easily re-run locally. We will publish a perf "
+"website to show the performance test results for each pull request."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- **Accuracy test**: We are working on adding accuracy test to the CI as "
+"well."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- **Nightly test**: we'll run full test every night to make sure the code is"
+" working."
+msgstr ""
+

+msgid "### 12. How to handle the out-of-memory issue?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- **Limit `--max-model-len`**: It can save the on-chip memory usage for KV "
+"cache initialization step."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- **Adjust `--gpu-memory-utilization`**: If unspecified, the default value "
+"is `0.9`. You can decrease this value to reserve more memory to reduce "
+"fragmentation risks. See details in: [vLLM - Inference and Serving - Engine "
+"Arguments](https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/cli/serve/#-gpu-memory-"
+"utilization)."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- **Configure `PYTORCH_NPU_ALLOC_CONF`**: Set this environment variable to "
+"optimize NPU memory management. For example, you can use `export "
+"PYTORCH_NPU_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True` to enable virtual memory "
+"feature to mitigate memory fragmentation caused by frequent dynamic memory "
+"size adjustments during runtime. See details in "
+"[PYTORCH_NPU_ALLOC_CONF](https://www.hiascend.com/document/detail/zh/Pytorch/700/comref/Envvariables/Envir_012.html)."
+msgstr ""

+msgid ""
+"### 16. How to fix the error \"ImportError: Please install vllm[audio] for "
+"audio support\" for the multi-modal models？"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Some multi-modal models requires the `librosa` package to be installed, you need to install the `qwen-omni-utils` package to ensure all dependencies are met, for Qwen-omni, run `pip install qwen-omni-utils`.\n"
+"This package will install `librosa` and its related dependencies, resolving the `ImportError: No module named 'librosa'` issue and ensuring that the audio processing functionality works correctly."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"### 17. How to troubleshoot and resolve size capture failures resulting from"
+" stream resource exhaustion, and what are the underlying causes?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. Upgrade to a newer HDK/CANN stack if one is available for your environment. Recent releases improve ACL graph capacity, so older workarounds may no longer be necessary.\n"
+"2. Manually reduce the configured graph sizes, for example: '{\"cudagraph_capture_sizes\":[size1, size2, size3, ...]}', or lower `max_cudagraph_capture_size`.\n"
+"3. If your workload is mostly uniform decode, try ACLGraph's `FULL` or `FULL_DECODE_ONLY` mode instead of the `PIECEWISE`.\n"
+"4. If you use `PIECEWISE` or `FULL_AND_PIECEWISE` and still hit this failure after upgrading, set `cudagraph_capture_sizes` manually according to your real workload and reduce the configured coverage.\n"
+"5. If you are debugging a startup failure, temporarily disable graph mode (`cudagraph_mode=\"NONE\"` / `enforce_eager=True`) to confirm the issue is capture-related."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"Root cause analysis:\n"
+"ACL graph capture can still fail when the runtime resources required by the selected graph sizes exceed what the current software/hardware stack can provide. This is most visible in `PIECEWISE` scenarios because the number of captured graphs scales with model depth and capture-size coverage. vLLM Ascend no longer auto-shrinks the PIECEWISE capture-size set locally, so the practical mitigations are to upgrade the HDK/CANN stack or reduce the configured graph sizes explicitly. The runtime guidance is intentionally narrow: it is only added when capture fails with the confirmed stream-resource signature above."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 18. How to install custom version of torch_npu?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"### 19. On certain systems (e.g., Kylin OS), `docker pull` may fail with an "
+"`invalid tar header` error"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"1. On a separate host machine (e.g., a standard Ubuntu server), pull the "
+"image for the target ARM64 architecture and package it into a `.tar` file."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "2. Transfer the image archive"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"### 20. Why am I getting an error when executing the script to start a "
+"Docker container? The error message is: \"operation not permitted\""
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"### 21. How to set `SOC_VERSION` when building from source on a CPU-only "
+"machine?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 22. Why TPOT increases drastically as concurrency grows?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"When testing a vLLM server, one may find that TPOT increases as concurrency increases (for example, TPOT increases by 0.5 ~ 1ms when concurrency increases by 4). This phenomenon is normal in most cases. However, sometimes TPOT may increase dramatically (10 to 100ms for example) as concurrency grows. This is possibly caused by [**PREEMPTION**](https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/configuration/optimization/#preemption) in vLLM.\n"
+"Generally, when your server hits KV cache limits, vLLM tries to free KV cache of requests to ensure sufficient space for other requests, which is called preemption in vLLM. When a request is preempted, the default behavior is to recompute the KV cache of this request again in the future, which is why the performance might drop significantly. There are several ways to verify this:"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- vLLM usually logs stats on your server. You might see metrics like `GPU KV cache usage: 99.0%,`. When reaching 100%, it triggers preemption.\n"
+"- When launching a vLLM server, you will see logs like `GPU KV cache size: 66340 tokens` and `Maximum concurrency for 16,384 tokens per request: 4.05`. These are estimated KV cache capacity for a single DP group. You can adjust the overall request traffic according to this."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 23. How do I choose between single-node and multi-node deployment?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 24. What quantization method should I use?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"- **BF16**: Best accuracy, highest memory footprint. Use for accuracy-critical applications or when memory is sufficient.\n"
+"- **W8A8**: Good balance of accuracy and memory reduction. Use for large models (e.g., 32B) on memory-constrained hardware.\n"
+"- **W4A8/W4A4**: Maximum memory reduction. Suitable for deploying larger models on smaller hardware configurations, with some accuracy trade-off."
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid "### 25. When should I enable FlashComm_v1?"
+msgstr ""
+
+msgid ""
+"### 26. What is the difference between FIA and PA operators for attention?"
 msgstr ""
-"FIA (Flash Attention) 是 vLLM-Ascend 中的默认注意力算子。在某些批次大小设置下（特别是中等并发），FIA 可能表现出次优性能。PA (Page Attention) 算子可以通过 `--additional-config` 中的 `pa_shape_list` 手动启用。当运行时批次大小与 `pa_shape_list` 中的值匹配时，框架会切换到 PA。这是一个临时的调优参数——未来的 FIA 优化将使此参数过时。"

-#~ " piece graph count, multistream-overlap "

-#~ "operator characteristics and specific hardware"

-#~ "resulting in stream resource exhaustion "
-#~ "during size capture operations."
